knowledge_base.title: Dassam Fall | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page provides information about Dassam Fall, a popular waterfall near Ranchi in Jharkhand. It describes the location—about 34 km from Ranchi on the Ranchi‑Tata road near Taimara village—notes that the fall is fed by the River Kachni and drops from a height of 144 feet, and mentions the distinctive sight of ten separate water streams.  

A “How to Reach” section outlines travel options: the nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport (≈31 km away), the closest railway station is Ranchi Railway Station (≈26 km away), and the Birsa Munda Bus Stand at Kantatoli is about 24 km from the site.  

The page also includes a photo gallery with images of the waterfall and a link to view all photos, plus social‑media sharing buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter). Overall, the content serves as a visitor‑friendly guide to Dassam Fall, offering practical travel details and visual insight into the attraction.

knowledge_base.title: Privacy Policy for Booth Status App | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Privacy Policy for the Booth Status App – Ranchi District, Government of Jharkhand**

This page explains how the Booth Status mobile application (available on the NIC e‑Gov Mobile App Store in Google Play) handles the information of its users.

**Purpose of the App**  
The Booth Status App is an e‑governance tool that delivers government services and information to citizens. It provides general reference material supplied by various Ministries, Departments, or allied government bodies. While the app strives for accuracy, users should verify any critical details with the concerned authorities, as the information may not carry legal weight.

**What Information Is Collected**

1. **User‑Provided Information**  
   - The app does not require registration to use its features.  
   - If you choose to share personal details (e.g., for receiving notifications), that information may be used to contact you with important updates or notices.

2. **Automatically Collected Information**  
   - Device type, unique device ID, IP address, mobile operating system, and browser type.  
   - Usage patterns within the app.  
   - Location data obtained via GPS (or similar technology) to identify your city and display a location map. This location information may be transmitted to government authorities for operational or policy‑making purposes.

**How Your Information May Be Used**  
- To deliver the app’s core functions and improve service delivery.  
- To send you relevant notifications or updates, should you have opted to receive them.  
- To support location‑based features (e.g., showing nearby booths or service points).  
- To comply with legal obligations, such as responding to subpoenas or other lawful requests.

**Your Controls**  
If you prefer not to share your location, you can disable location services for the app through your phone’s settings or within the app itself. Note that turning off location may disable any features that rely on GPS data.

**Disclosure**  
The app may share both user‑provided and automatically collected data with government entities when required by law or to fulfill its service objectives.

**Summary for Citizens**  
The Booth Status App is designed to make government services more accessible. While it collects basic device and usage data—and optionally location information—to function effectively and improve public service delivery, it does not mandate registration. Users retain control over location sharing and are encouraged to verify any critical information obtained through the app with the appropriate government offices.

knowledge_base.title: Jaano Local Covid Emergency System | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page introduces the **Jaano Local Covid Emergency System**, a mobile‑based platform set up by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand) to support citizens during the COVID‑19 pandemic.  

**What the system does**

1. **Location‑based information feeds** – delivers real‑time updates and alerts relevant to the user’s geographic area.  
2. **Location‑based emergency services and hospital information** – helps users locate nearby medical facilities, testing centres, and emergency contacts.  
3. **Dissemination of important information and collection of user surveys** – shares official advisories, guidelines, and health‑related notices while gathering feedback from the public to improve response efforts.

**Resources provided on the page**

- A downloadable **Application features and manual** (PDF) that explains how the app works and what functions it offers.  
- A direct link to the **Jaano Android app** on the Google Play Store for easy installation.

Overall, the page serves as a citizen‑friendly gateway to access the Jaano app, understand its capabilities, and obtain the necessary documentation to use the system for COVID‑19 related emergency assistance and information.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “Past Notices → Recruitment” section on the Ranchi district website serves as an archive of all recruitment‑related announcements that have been issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. It allows citizens to view, download, and refer to earlier notices, results, counselling schedules, eligibility lists, and other documents connected with government job selections and related processes.

**Key content presented**  

- A table that lists each past recruitment notice in chronological order (most recent at the top).  
- For every entry the table provides:  

  1. **Title** – a brief headline describing the notice (e.g., “Result under NHM Adv 1/2025”, “Counselling Process for 16 Selected Candidates for Graduate Trained Teacher, (6 to 8) and Inter Trained (1 to 5)”, “Advertisement for the appointment of sports trainers …”).  
  2. **Description** – a short summary of what the notice contains (press releases, office orders, short‑listed candidate lists, counselling details, eligible/not‑eligible applicant lists, exam schedules, etc.).  
  3. **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which the notice was valid or the activity was scheduled (dates range from 25 September 2025 to 17 February 2026).  
  4. **File** – one or more downloadable PDF links (hosted on the government’s s3waas domain) that contain the full notice, annexures, candidate lists, check‑slips, or related documents.  

- The notices cover a variety of recruitment activities, including:  

  * Results and office orders for National Health Mission (NHM) advertisements.  
  * Counselling processes for selected candidates in teaching positions (Graduate Trained Teacher, Inter Trained, Sahayak Acharya, etc.).  
  * Lists of eligible and ineligible applicants for specific posts (e.g., Day‑Boarding Coach) together with scheduled dates for physical tests and interviews.  
  * Notices for physical and written examinations for Home Guard recruitment.  
  * Advertisements inviting applications for sports‑trainer vacancies in day‑boarding sports‑training centres.  
  * Information on the distribution of appointment letters for posts such as Chowkidar.  
  * Details regarding admission of BPL‑family students in recognized private schools under the Right to Education (RTE) Act.  

- Each PDF file is labeled with its size (e.g., “View (1 MB)”, “Candidate List (347 KB)”) to help users gauge the download size before opening it.

**What a citizen can do here**  
By browsing this table, a resident of Ranchi can locate a specific past recruitment notice, verify the timeline of a selection process, download the official PDF for reference, and check details such as exam dates, counselling schedules, or result announcements without needing to visit the office in person. The page thus functions as a transparent, easily accessible repository of the district’s recruitment history.

knowledge_base.title: Zila Gazat Praptra-23(K) and Praptra-23(KH) of RMC 2026 and BNP 2026 | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This notice from the District Ranchi office of the Government of Jharkhand announces the publication of two sets of gazette notifications—**Zila Gazat Praptra‑23(K)** and **Praptra‑23(KH)**—for the years 2026, relating to the Ranchi Municipal Corporation (RMC) and the BNP (Bokaro Nagar Parishad/equivalent local body).  

The notice specifies that the notifications are effective from **6 March 2026** to **6 April 2026**. It provides direct download links to four PDF documents that contain the full text of the notices:

- **View (91 KB)** – a combined overview of the notices.  
- **Zila Gazat 2026 Praptra‑23(k) RMC Ward (104 KB)** – the RMC‑ward‑specific Praptra‑23(K).  
- **Zila Gazat 2026 Praptra‑23(k) BNP Ward (94 KB)** – the BNP‑ward‑specific Praptra‑23(K).  
- **Zila Gazat 2026 Praptra‑23(kh) RMC Mayor (91 KB)** – the RMC‑mayor‑level Praptra‑23(KH).  

By making these PDFs available, the page enables citizens, officials, and other stakeholders to view, verify, and download the official gazette entries for the stated period. The notice also includes standard sharing options (Facebook, X) and a breadcrumb trail showing its location under the “Announcements” section of the Ranchi district website.

knowledge_base.title: Forest & Environment Ranchi, Jharkhand | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page is the official Forest & Environment section for Ranchi district, Jharkhand. Its main purpose is to inform citizens about the District Environment Plan prepared for Ranchi and to invite public participation.

Key content includes:

- A clear heading “Forest & Environment Ranchi, Jharkhand.”
- A brief announcement that a **District Environment Plan of Ranchi District** has been prepared.
- A direct link titled **“Environment Plan Ranchi”** (https://ranchi.nic.in/notice/district-environment-plan-of-ranchi-district/) where the plan can be viewed or downloaded.
- A note encouraging all residents of Ranchi district to submit their suggestions or comments on the plan via the email address **dfo-ranchi@gov.in**.

The page also contains standard navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, social‑share buttons for Facebook and X) but does not list any additional directories, contacts, or tender information. It serves as an informational hub for the district’s environment planning process and a channel for public feedback.

knowledge_base.title: Contact Us | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The “Contact Us” page for the District Ranchi website explains that the site was created and is maintained by the National Informatics Center, Ranchi, with content overseen by the District Administration. It provides the email addresses for the Web Information Manager (ac‑ranchi@jharkhandmail.gov.in and jhrran@nic.in) for any queries about the website, invites users to submit feedback on content, design, or technology through the site’s feedback section, and lists the physical mailing address for the Web Information Manager: Block‑A, DC Office, Kutchery Chowk, Ranchi‑834001, Jharkhand. The page also includes social‑share links for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) to let visitors share the contact‑us page.

knowledge_base.title: विज्ञापन सूचना | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is a recruitment notice issued by the District Child Protection Unit (जिला बाल संरक्षण इकाई) in Ranchi, Jharkhand. It announces a vacancy for the position of “सहायक व्यक्ति” (Assistant Person). The notice provides the essential details applicants need to know:

- **Position:** Assistant Person (सहायक व्यक्ति)  
- **Unit:** District Child Protection Unit, Ranchi  
- **Application Period:** Starts on 23 March 2026 and ends on 25 April 2026  
- **Document:** A PDF file (approximately 3 MB) containing the full advertisement can be viewed or downloaded via the link provided.  

The notice is presented in Hindi and includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumbs, social‑share buttons) typical of the district government website. No additional contact information or lists of entities are included beyond the recruitment details shown above.

knowledge_base.title: बैंक |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as a public‑utility directory for banking services available in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It helps residents locate nearby bank branches and obtain basic contact information (email addresses) for each listed institution.

**Key content**  

- **Bank listings** – The page presents a series of bank entries, each displayed in a boxed section with the bank’s name as a heading.  
- **Location links** – For every bank, a clickable link opens a Google‑Maps search that points to the general area where the branch is situated (e.g., “कटारुका हाउस रांची”, “रातू रोड रांची”, “रांची मेन रोड”).  
- **Contact email** – Beneath each map link, the page provides an email address (masked with “[at]” and “[dot]” to avoid spam) for the respective bank, such as:  
  - आईडीबीआई बैंक – somenath_kundu[at]idbi[dot]co[dot]in  
  - आईसीआईसीआई बैंक – rajasekhar[dot]babu[at]icicibank[dot]com  
  - इंडसइंड बैंक – amit[dot]skumar[at]indusind[dot]com  
  - एचडीएफसी – (an email address is shown in the HTML snippet, though the text is cut off)  

The directory continues with additional banks (e.g., एसबीआई, पंजाब नेशनल बैंक, आदि), each following the same pattern of a name, a map‑search link, and an email contact.

**What a citizen can do**  
A resident can click the map link to see the approximate location of a bank branch on Google Maps, then use the provided email address to reach out for inquiries about services, account opening, or other banking needs. This makes the page a handy reference for locating and contacting banks in Ranchi.

knowledge_base.title: ANIMAL HUSBANDRY | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The Animal Husbandry page for Ranchi district outlines the role of the Animal Husbandry sector within the Department of Agriculture, Animal Husbandry and Cooperation. It explains that the sector oversees livestock production, disease prevention, stock improvement, and dairy development, as well as fisheries—both inland and marine. The page highlights how dairying serves as a supplementary income source for small and marginal farmers and agricultural laborers, helps improve community nutrition, and can drive socio‑economic change in rural areas. Likewise, it notes that fisheries development leverages Jharkhand’s extensive inland water resources to enhance food security, create employment, raise rural incomes, and support socio‑economic transformation through fish farming as a primary or secondary livelihood. The content is aimed at informing citizens about the functions and benefits of animal husbandry and fisheries programs in the district.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as an archive of past tender and procurement notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It lists a variety of government‑issued opportunities that were open for bidding during late 2021 and early 2022, allowing citizens to view what projects were previously advertised and to download the associated documents.

The notices cover several sectors:

* **Education & awareness** – a request for proposals (RFP) to create pandemic‑sensitive comic books for children, together with a corrigendum to that RFP.  
* **Health & nutrition** – an expression of interest (EOI) for a mobile nutrition van to serve Ranchi.  
* **Sports & recreation** – a very short‑term tender for regular food supply at the residential badminton centre in Khelgaon, and an EOI for setting up an open‑air gym under the District Mineral Foundation Trust.  
* **Infrastructure & facilities** – EOIs for establishing a STEM laboratory in the district, for selecting an agency to run the District Grievance Redressal Centre (including a corrigendum to that EOI), and for a short‑term tender in Mandar, Ranchi.  

Each entry provides the title, a brief description, the start and end dates of the bidding period, and a direct link to download the corresponding PDF notice (file sizes range from under 100 KB to several megabytes). The dates span from 18 December 2021 to 26 February 2022, showing a recent window of procurement activity.

By presenting these past notices in a searchable table, the page helps residents, businesses, and NGOs stay informed about the types of projects the district undertakes and the documentation that supported those procurements.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as an archive of past tender notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. It presents a table‑style list of completed procurement opportunities, allowing citizens to view what goods, services or works were previously advertised, when the bidding periods were open, and to download the original tender documents.

Each entry includes:

* **Title** – a brief name of the tender (e.g., “Short term tender invitation for educational tour of meritorious students”, “Book Tender for State Library Ranchi”, “Deaf & Dumb School Tender – Food & Daily Use Items”, “Tender notice for purchase of Blankets”, etc.).
* **Description** – a short elaboration of what the tender covered (such as supplying books and study materials, providing food items for a special school, or organizing an educational tour).
* **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which interested bidders could submit their offers (shown in DD/MM/YYYY format).
* **File** – a link to the PDF version of the tender notice (file sizes are indicated, ranging from a few hundred kilobytes to several megabytes), which can be opened or downloaded directly.

The tenders span various sectors—education, library services, social welfare (special schools), health‑related supplies (blankets), and recruitment‑related testing—reflecting the diverse procurement activities of the district administration. By consulting this archive, citizens can gain insight into the types of projects the district has undertaken, the timelines involved, and access the full tender notices for reference or transparency purposes.

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as an archive of past recruitment notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It presents a table‑style list of completed hiring processes, each entry showing the position title, a brief description, the period during which applications were accepted (start and end dates), and a link to the official PDF notice where full details—such as eligibility criteria, application procedure, and any subsequent results—can be viewed.

The notices cover a range of government‑sector opportunities, including:

- Paramedical staff recruitment  
- Vacancy for a Training Coordinator under the PMAY‑G (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin) project management unit  
- Lists of candidates eligible for skill tests for various MGNREGA roles (Assistant Engineer, Junior Engineer, Accounts Assistant, Computer Assistant)  
- Written‑test and interview announcements for drinking‑water and sanitation positions  
- Results of MGNREGA vacancy selections  
- Recruitment under the Drinking Water and Sanitation Department (DWSD) for specific projects (Prakalp 1)  
- Selections for the STLS (State Level Technical Support) post  
- Interview notices and short‑listed candidate details for the Tuberculosis (TB) Department  
- Guidelines and admit‑card PDFs for written tests related to the RNTCP (Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme)

Each row provides the exact dates the notice was active (e.g., Paramedical Recruitment from 11 January 2019 to 28 February 2019) and a downloadable PDF file (size indicated in kilobytes) that contains the complete notice. Citizens can browse this archive to see what recruitment drives have previously taken place in Ranchi district and access the original documents for reference or record‑keeping purposes.

knowledge_base.title: इ-राजस्व न्यायालय | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose: This page describes the E‑Revenue Court (इ‑राजस्व न्यायालय) service offered by the Ranchi district administration in Jharkhand. It explains that the court handles revenue‑related matters online and provides a direct link to its official website (http://jharerevenuecourt.nic.in/). The page also lists the physical location of the court’s office—Block‑A, Upper Collector Office, Collectorate Building, Ranchi, PIN 834001—so citizens know where to go for in‑person assistance. Breadcrumbs show the page’s place within the site (Home → Services → E‑Revenue Court), and social‑share icons let users share the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter). The purpose is to inform residents about the E‑Revenue Court service, how to access it online, and where to find the office if needed.

knowledge_base.title: रॉक गार्डन | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose: This page is the Rock Garden photo‑gallery section of the Ranchi district website (Government of Jharkhand). It presents a single image of the Rock Garden in Ranchi, accompanied by the gallery title “रॉक गार्डन” and a brief caption. The layout includes navigation breadcrumbs (Home → Photo Gallery → Rock Garden), options to switch between grid and list views, and social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) that let visitors share the page. The image is hosted on the government’s CDN and can be viewed in a lightbox when clicked. The page’s purpose is to showcase the Rock Garden attraction to citizens and visitors through a visual gallery.

knowledge_base.title: Photo Gallery | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The page serves as a visual directory of notable sites and attractions in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It presents a grid‑style photo gallery where each thumbnail links to a dedicated gallery page for a specific location. Visitors can click on images to view additional photos of places such as Hundru Fall, Rock Garden, Tagore Hill, Dassam Fall, Jonha Fall, general Ranchi scenery, waterfalls along Patratu Road, and a collection of tourist spots. The layout also includes simple navigation aids—breadcrumb links, social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter), and view‑switcher toggles for grid or list display—making it easy for citizens to browse and explore the district’s natural and cultural highlights.

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an archive of past announcements issued by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand). It presents a table‑style list of recent notices, each showing the notice title, a brief description, the period during which the notice was active (start and end dates), and a downloadable file (usually a PDF) that contains the full document.

The notices cover a range of government activities, including:

- Publication of a provisional seniority list for clerks in secondary and +2 schools, inviting review within a specified window.  
- Several “Very Short Tender” notices for the supply of materials and fruit plants under the Birsa Harit Gram Yojana scheme, with clear submission deadlines.  
- A notice detailing the availability of educational qualifications and allocated funds for 25 Ashrams, secondary schools, Kasturba Gandhi Hostels and Model Ashrams in the Ranchi region.  
- The release of a triple‑test survey report on backward‑class reservation for municipal elections, together with information on how voters can lodge objections.  
- Announcements related to the Ranchi Nagar Nigam (municipal corporation) election 2025, such as the publication of booth lists.  
- Recruitment advertisements, for example, for an assistant teacher position at a minority‑aided school.  
- A tender for the supply of digital smart boards under the State Level Akanksha Programme aimed at improving classroom teaching.  
- An expression of interest (EOI) inviting insurance providers to offer group coverage to Anganwadi workers and helpers under the Women, Child Development and Social Security Department.

Each entry includes the file size (e.g., 7 MB, 2 MB, 4 MB) and a direct link to the PDF, allowing citizens to view or download the complete notice for reference or action. The page thus functions as a transparent, easily navigable repository of recent district‑level public notices.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as the official “Voter Portal” for Ranchi district, providing citizens with easy access to voter‑related services and contact information for the local election office.

**Key content presented**

- **Link to the National Voter Service Portal (NVSP)** – a clickable URL (`http://www.nvsp.in/`) that directs users to the central platform for voter registration, electoral roll searches, application for voter ID cards, and other election‑related services.  
- **Contact details of the District Election Office** – the office is located in Block A, Room No‑312 of the Samaharnalay Building in Ranchi, with the postal PIN 833999.  
- **Breadcrumb navigation** – shows the hierarchical path: Home → Services → Voter Portal, helping users understand where they are within the district website.  
- **Social‑share options** – icons for sharing the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).  

Overall, the page acts as a gateway for Ranchi residents to reach national voter services and to find the physical location and contact information of the district’s election office for any in‑person assistance.

The page also includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, social‑share buttons for Facebook and X) to help users move around the site and share the information. No additional contact details or listings are presented.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page lists past tender notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It serves as an archive where citizens can view completed or cancelled procurement opportunities that were advertised by various government offices in the district.

Each entry in the table shows:

* **Title of the notice** – a brief description of the work or service being procured (e.g., “EOI Integrated Grievance Management Single Window Platform for Ranchi District,” “Tender Invitation for Supply and Installation of Whiteboards in Schools,” “Winter Uniform for AWC Children,” etc.).
* **Description** – a short elaboration of the scope, such as the specific items to be supplied, the location of work, or the purpose of the tender.
* **Start and End Dates** – the period during which the tender was open for submission (all dates are in DD/MM/YYYY format). For example, the grievance‑management platform EOI ran from 26 December 2024 to 6 January 2025, while the winter‑uniform tender was open only on 25 – 26 September 2024.
* **File link** – a downloadable PDF (size indicated in MB or KB) that contains the full tender notice, terms, and any attached documents. Links point to the government’s secure storage domain (cdn.s3waas.gov.in).

The notices cover a range of sectors, including education (whiteboards for schools, district education officer supplies), health and child welfare (winter uniforms for Anganwadi children), infrastructure (CCTV installation from MLA funds, LED lighting and equipment for an indoor badminton stadium), community services (kiosk allotment for minority communities, operation of an Ashram school), and administrative processes (grievance‑management platform, sweep‑banner tender cancellation, short‑term tenders for the Sveep cell).

By providing the title, description, timeline, and direct PDF access, the page enables residents, businesses, and interested parties to review what procurements have recently been concluded in Ranchi district, understand the types of projects the administration undertakes, and access the original tender documents for reference or transparency purposes.

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as a public archive of recent announcements issued by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand). It presents a table‑style list of notices, each showing the notice title, a brief description, the period during which the notice is valid (start and end dates), and a direct link to download the associated PDF document.  

Key notices include:

* **Result under NHM Adv 1/2025** – press release, office order and short‑listed candidate list (valid 18 Jan 2026 – 17 Feb 2026).  
* **Birth & Death registration for November 2025** – district‑level vital‑statistics report (valid 27 Dec 2025 – 27 Jan 2026).  
* **26 Jan 2026 JHANKI (Baal Vivah Mukt Jharkhand)** – awareness programme on child‑marriage prevention (valid 16 Jan 2026 – 23 Jan 2026).  
* **Posting details of newly appointed Sahayak Aachrya** – deployment order for newly hired assistant teachers (valid 24 Dec 2025 – 08 Jan 2026).  
* **Counselling process for 16 selected candidates** – schedule and documents for graduate‑trained and inter‑trained teacher placements (valid 27 Dec 2025 – 29 Dec 2025).  
* **Book tender for State Library, Ranchi** – invitation to supply books and study materials (valid 17 Dec 2025 – 24 Dec 2025).  
* **Advertisement for District Level Youth Festival‑2025** – cultural competitions (folk dance, song, poetry, painting, declamation) to be held on 20 Dec 2025 (valid 13 Dec 2025 – 23 Dec 2025).  
* **Reference to Advertisement No. 415/2025** – eligible/not‑eligible list and schedule for physical test & interview for Day‑Boarding Coach posts (test on 16 Dec 2025).  
* **Information on private schools applying for RTE recognition** – status of applications for Right‑to‑Education compliance.  
* **DEAF & DUMB SCHOOL TENDER** – procurement of food and daily‑use items for the special‑needs school.  

Each entry provides a clickable “View” link that opens the corresponding PDF (file sizes range from a few hundred kilobytes to several megabytes), allowing citizens to download the full notice for reference or action. The page thus functions as a convenient, up‑to‑date bulletin board for administrative results, public‑service updates, event announcements, tender opportunities, and recruitment‑related information relevant to residents of Ranchi district.

knowledge_base.title: प्रारंभिक अधिसूचना | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page publishes a *preliminary notification* (प्रारंभिक अधिसूचना) issued by the District Collector, Ranchi, concerning the acquisition of land for the construction of the Karanji reservoir project.

**Key information contained in the notice**

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| **Subject** | Land acquisition for the Karanji reservoir project |
| **Location** | Gram‑Karanji, Thana‑Bedo, Thana No. 49, Anchal‑Bedo, District Ranchi |
| **Area to be acquired** | 0.17 acre (≈ 0.068 hectare) of standard‑measure land |
| **Notice reference** | Preliminary Notification No. 52, dated 16 February 2026 |
| **Publication period** | Starts 18 February 2026 – ends 18 March 2026 |
| **Supporting document** | PDF file (≈ 1 MB) available for download/viewing: <https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/s32b8a61594b1f4c4db0902a8a395ced93/uploads/2026/02/17713930942006.pdf> |

**What a citizen should know**  
The notice informs residents that the government intends to acquire the specified parcel of land for the Karanji reservoir. Interested parties can review the full details in the attached PDF and have until 18 March 2026 to submit any objections or suggestions during the stipulated notice period. The page also provides standard sharing options (Facebook, X) and a breadcrumb trail for easy navigation back to the district’s notice section.

knowledge_base.title: Pahari Mandir, Ranchi | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as an informational guide for citizens and tourists interested in visiting the Pahari Mandir (Lord Shiva temple) in Ranchi. It presents the temple’s significance, practical details for reaching it, and visual media to help visitors plan their trip.

**Key content presented**

- **Description** – The temple is described as an ancient shrine of Lord Shankara (Shiva) situated on a hill called Pahari Mandir, a bare black‑rock outcrop in the middle of flat terrain.
- **Location & accessibility** – It lies about 8 km from Ranchi railway station and 12 km from the airport. The hill rises to 2,140 feet, and visitors must climb 468 steps to reach the summit.
- **Visuals** – A set of images (hosted on the government’s CDN) showcases the temple and its surroundings.
- **Multimedia** – An embedded YouTube video provides a virtual tour or additional information about the site.
- **Official link** – A prominent button directs users to the temple’s official website (paharimandirranchi.com) for further details.
- **Social sharing** – Ready‑to‑use buttons allow visitors to share the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

Overall, the page consolidates essential visitor information—description, distance, access steps, images, video, and official resources—into a single, easily accessible government‑hosted tourism resource.

knowledge_base.title: RANCHI COMMISSIONARY | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The Ranchi Commissionary page explains that the Ranchi (South Chhotanagpur) Division comprises five districts—Ranchi, Lohardaga, Gumla, Simdega, and Khunti. It presents a table that gives, for each district, the name of the Deputy Commissioner (with a link to the district’s official website), the Superintendent of Police, and a direct link to the district’s website. This information lets citizens quickly see which administrative and police officials oversee each district and where they can find more detailed district‑level resources. The page also includes a brief description of the division’s headquarters and its official name, reinforcing the administrative structure for visitors seeking contact details or general knowledge about the region.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an archive of past recruitment notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It presents a table‑style listing of notices that were published between September and November 2019, each entry showing the notice title, a brief description, the start and end dates for applications or related activities, and a link to the downloadable PDF (with file‑size information).

Key notices include:

- A contractual recruitment drive for Junior Engineer and Account Clerk‑cum‑Computer Operator under the 14th Finance Commission (application window 25 Oct – 30 Nov 2019, PDF ≈ 760 KB).  
- Results and related documents for the National Health Mission (NHM), including result sheets and supplementary files (early Nov 2019, PDFs ≈ 327 KB–562 KB).  
- The outcome of an Expression of Interest for Sadar Hospital (early Nov 2019, PDF ≈ 438 KB).  
- Vacancy announcements for Senior Treatment Supervisor, Lab Technician, Counsellor (for DB‑TB) and District Pharmacist (mid‑Oct – early Nov 2019, with multiple attachments totaling roughly 1 MB).  
- NHM requirement notices calling for interview and skill‑test schedules (late Oct 2019, PDF ≈ 1 MB).  
- A recruitment notice for a Yoga Trainer at Health and Wellness Centres (late Oct 2019, PDF ≈ 6 MB).  
- Admit cards for the Junior Engineer and Account Clerk‑cum‑Computer Operator posts (early Oct 2019, PDFs ≈ 164 KB–512 KB).  
- Information regarding interviews and computer‑proficiency tests for vacant posts under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana‑Rural (mid‑Oct 2019).  
- A notice for hiring a District Program Manager on a contract basis (mid‑Oct 2019).  
- An MGNREGA recruitment notice for Ranchi (late Sep 2019).

Overall, the page provides citizens with a transparent record of past government recruitment opportunities in Ranchi, allowing them to view the official notices, check important dates, and download the associated PDF documents for reference.

knowledge_base.title: घोषणाएँ |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
The “घोषणाएँ” (Announcements) section of the Ranchi district website serves as a public notice board where the Jharkhand government posts official updates for citizens. Each entry includes a concise title, a detailed description, the start and end dates for the notice’s relevance, and a downloadable PDF file that contains the full information.

The current listings cover a range of topics:

- **Job Fair (रोजगार मेला)** – Announces a recruitment event for job seekers, scheduled for 14 March 2026, with a 594 KB PDF providing further details.  
- **Short‑term Tender Notice for RFE (आरएफई के लिए अल्पकालिक निविदा सूचना)** – Invites bids from reputable agencies for the supply and installation of post‑harvest management components under the state horticulture scheme, open from 9 March 2026 to 14 March 2026; the accompanying PDF is about 1 MB.  
- **Tender Invitation (निविदा आमंत्रण)** – Seeks proposals for upgrading education facilities in Ranchi’s government schools, including ICT labs, libraries, and learning‑cum‑recreation centres; the tender runs from 9 March 2026 to 27 March 2026 and is detailed in an 8 MB PDF.  
- **Declaration (अधिघोषणा)** – Concerns the acquisition of land for the Karanji reservoir project in Karanji village, Bero block, Ranchi district, specifying the total area (6.56 acres / 2.655 hectares) and providing a 2 MB PDF with the full notice.  
- Additional notices include tenders for school infrastructure, RTE‑Act related announcements, and electoral booth lists, each with its own date range and PDF attachment (file sizes ranging from a few hundred kilobytes to several megabytes).

Overall, the page aggregates timely, official communications—job fairs, procurement opportunities, land‑acquisition declarations, and other civic updates—allowing residents to view, download, and act on the information as needed.

knowledge_base.title: गांव और पंचायत | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page “गांव और पंचायत” (Villages and Panchayats) on the Ranchi district website presents an overview of the rural administrative structure within the district. It begins with a brief statement that Ranchi district comprises 1,311 villages grouped into 305 panchayats.

Following this introduction, a table lists each of the district’s 18 blocks (also referred to as “ब्लॉक नाम”) along with two key figures for every block:

* **पंचायतों की संख्या** – the number of panchayats operating in the block  
* **गांवों की संख्या** – the number of villages that fall under those panchayats  

The blocks covered are:

1. अनगड़ा – 21 panchayats, 83 villages  
2. बेड़ो – 17 panchayats, 84 villages  
3. बुंडू – 11 panchayats, 88 villages  
4. बुडमू – 14 panchayats, 77 villages  
5. चाँनो – 17 panchayats, 67 villages  
6. इटकी – 9 panchayats, 46 villages  
7. कांके – 32 panchayats, 135 villages  
8. खलारी – 14 panchayats, 25 villages  
9. लापुंग – 11 panchayats, 79 villages  
10. मांडर – 19 panchayats, 69 villages  
11. नगड़ी – 13 panchayats, 46 villages  
12. नामकुम – 23 panchayats, 110 villages  
13. ओरमांझी – 18 panchayats, 91 villages  
14. राहे – 9 panchayats, 59 villages  
15. रातू – 20 panchayats, 51 villages  
16. सिल्ली – 20 panchayats, 102 villages  
17. सोनाहातु – 14 panchayats, 64 villages  
18. तमाड़ – 23 panchayats, 144 villages  

The purpose of the page is to inform citizens about how the district’s rural areas are organized into panchayats and villages, providing a clear, block‑wise breakdown that can be useful for understanding local governance, planning community interactions, or accessing services tied to specific panchayats or villages. No additional contact details, dates, or other entities are presented on this page.

knowledge_base.title: Administrative Setup | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page explains how the Ranchi district administration is organized and who is responsible for its various functions. It serves as a quick reference for citizens who want to understand the hierarchy and the main bodies that handle revenue, development, law‑and‑order, elections, and local governance in the district.

**Key content**  

- **Leadership role** – The Deputy Commissioner heads the district administration and simultaneously holds the titles of Collector (revenue), District Development Commissioner (development works), District Magistrate (law‑and‑order and general administration), and District Election Officer (elections). The Deputy Commissioner is supported by Sub‑Divisional Magistrates who oversee each sub‑division.  

- **Administrative units** – The setup is broken down into the following components:  
  1. **Collectorate** – the central office handling revenue and general administration.  
  2. **Tehsil** – sub‑divisions for revenue and land‑record matters.  
  3. **Police** – law‑enforcement machinery under the district’s command.  
  4. **Subdivision & Blocks** – intermediate administrative tiers that manage development programs and local governance.  
  5. **Village & Panchayats** – grass‑roots units responsible for rural administration and service delivery.  
  6. **Constituencies** – electoral boundaries used for legislative and parliamentary elections.  

The information is presented in plain text with a brief introductory paragraph followed by a bullet‑point list, making it easy for residents to grasp who does what and where to approach for different civic needs.

knowledge_base.title: पुस्तक की आपूर्ति हेतु निविदा आमंत्रण  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page publishes a tender notice issued by the District Administration of Ranchi (Government of Jharkhand) inviting bids for the supply of books to various schools in the district. The procurement is to be funded through the MPLAD (Member of Parliament Local Area Development) scheme.  

Key details include:  

- **Tender title:** “पुस्तक की आपूर्ति हेतु निविदा आमंत्रण” (Invitation for Tender for Supply of Books)  
- **Description:** The tender seeks suppliers to provide books for different schools across the district using MPLAD funds.  
- **Important dates:**  
  - **Bid submission starts:** 14 March 2026  
  - **Last date for submission:** 27 March 2026  
- **Associated document:** A PDF file (≈823 KB) containing the full tender notice, available for download/viewing at the link provided.  

The notice is presented in Hindi and includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumbs, social‑share options) typical of the district’s official website. Citizens interested in participating can download the PDF, review the eligibility and submission requirements, and submit their bids before the stipulated deadline.

knowledge_base.title: Hospital |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as a directory of health‑care facilities in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It lists hospitals, private medical centres and blood‑bank units that are available to the public, giving each entry’s name, location (address and a Google‑Maps link), and contact details where provided.

- **Abdul Razzak Ansari Memorial Hospital** – Irba Post Office, NH 23, Rukka, Ranchi HO, Ranchi 834001; phone +91 651 227 5717.  
- **Alam Hospital and Research Centre Pvt. Ltd.** – Booti Road, Bariatu, Ranchi 834009; email info[at]alamhospital[dot]in; phone +91 651 254 7233; website http://alamhospital.in.  
- **Archi Jharkhand Blood Bank** – Near Petrol Pump, RIMS, Bariatu, Ranchi 834009.  
- **Blood Bank at Gandhi Nagar Hospital** – Gandhi Nagar, Kanke Road, Ranchi 834008.  
- **Blood Bank at H E C Hospital** – H E C Plant, Dhurwa, Ranchi 834004.  
- **Blood Bank at Nagarmal Modi Seva Sadan** – Seva Sadan Road, Upper Bazar, Ranchi 834001.  
- **Blood Banks RIMS** – Bariatu, Ranchi 834008.  
- **Buddhist Mission Hospital** – In front of B.Ed. College, Gautam Buddha Marg, Bariatu Road, Bariatu, Ranchi 834009.  
- **C C L Blood Bank** – C C L Hospital, Gandhinagar, Kanke Road, Ranchi 834008.  
- **Central Institute of Psychiatry** – Kanke, Ranchi 834006.

Each entry includes a clickable Google‑Maps link for easy navigation, and where available, phone numbers, email addresses, or website links are shown to help citizens reach the facilities directly. The page therefore functions as a practical reference for residents seeking medical services or blood‑bank locations in Ranchi.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
This page serves as an archive of past official notices and announcements issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It allows citizens to view, download, and refer to recent government communications related to recruitment, infrastructure projects, tenders, public services, and other administrative matters.

**Key content highlighted in the notices**

| Notice title | What it concerns | Important dates (start – end) | How to access the detail |
|--------------|------------------|------------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Notice regarding appointment of Chowkidar** | Final result of the Chowkidar examination (Advertisement No. 01/2024) | 20 Aug 2025 – 30 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 554 KB) – “View Result” link |
| **List of Candidates for Medical Test** | Candidates shortlisted for the medical test following the Chowkidar exam | 22 Aug 2025 – 30 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 3 MB) – two Google‑Drive links for the list |
| **Regarding publication of Section‑11** | Widening and strengthening work on the Banta‑Rahe, Bundu road | 01 Jul 2025 – 28 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 4 MB) |
| **Section‑19** | PCC (plain cement concrete) road construction in Timber Gali, Ward No. 01 | 29 Jul 2025 – 28 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 96 KB) |
| **Man power and material to be supplied by the agency/supplier for lodging** | Requirements for agencies/suppliers to provide manpower and materials for lodging arrangements | 14 Aug 2025 – 25 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 4 MB) |
| **Regarding Food & Cleaning Tender** | Tender for food and cleaning management for farmer‑training at the Extension Training Centre, Hehal, Ranchi | 11 Aug 2025 – 22 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 2 MB) |
| **Provisional application list for Excise Lottery Ranchi** | Provisional list of applicants for the Excise Lottery in Ranchi | 21 Aug 2025 – 22 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 7 MB) – includes a Google‑Drive view link |
| **Counseling Process for Candidate Selected for Sahayak Achrya (Lang) Subjects of Class (6 to 8)** | Details of the counseling procedure for selected candidates for language‑subject teaching assistants (classes 6‑8) | 12 Aug 2025 – 20 Aug 2025 | PDF (≈ 673 KB) plus supplementary guidelines, check‑slip, and candidate‑list files |
| **Regarding publication of advertisement related to employment fair** | Notice about publishing an advertisement for an upcoming employment fair | (dates not shown in the excerpt) | PDF link provided |
| **Notice related to Home Guard Application Error Correction – Ranchi District** | Instructions for applicants (Rural/Urban Home Guard recruitment, Advt. No. 01/2025) to correct errors in their online applications and submit required documents at the District Commandant Office, Dhurwa | (dates not shown in the excerpt) | PDF link provided |

**How a citizen can use this information**  
- **Check results or eligibility** – Download the Chowkidar result or medical‑test list to see if you have qualified.  
- **Stay informed about infrastructure works** – Review the Section

knowledge_base.title: Public Utilities | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The “Public Utilities” page on the Ranchi district website serves as a citizen‑friendly directory that groups essential services and institutions into easy‑to‑navigate tabs.  

When the **Bank** tab is opened, the page displays a list of major banks operating in Ranchi, giving each branch’s street address (e.g., “Ranchi Main Road” or “Upper Bazar Ranchi”) and, where available, a contact email formatted for the district’s site (such as ranchi[dot]branchhead[at]axisbank[dot]com for Axis Bank or UpperBazar[dot]Ranchi[at]bankofindia[dot]co[dot]in for Bank of India). Each entry also includes a link that opens the location in Google Maps, allowing users to view the exact spot and get directions.

The **College/University** tab presents higher‑education institutions in the district. For each college or university the page provides the full campus address, the official website (e.g., https://www.bitmesra.ac.in/ for Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra; http://bauranchi.org for Birsa Agricultural University; http://www.citranchi.ac.in/ for Cambridge Institute of Technology), and, when listed, an email address and phone number. As with the banks, a Google Maps link is supplied so citizens can locate the campus quickly.

Overall, the page consolidates contact details, web addresses, and map locations for banks and educational establishments (with additional tabs for electricity, hospitals, municipality, NGOs, and schools), offering residents a single, organized source to find and reach the public utilities they need.

knowledge_base.title: जनगणना डेटा 2011 |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose: This page serves as a public‑access repository for the 2011 Census data specific to Ranchi district in Jharkhand. Its main purpose is to allow citizens to view or download the official census dataset in PDF format. The page displays a single document titled “जनगणना डेटा 2011” (Census Data 2011), dated 27 April 2018, with a file size of 4 MB. A prominent “देखें (4 MB)” button links directly to the PDF hosted on the government’s CDN, enabling users to open or download the census information for reference or analysis. The layout includes a simple table showing the document’s title, date, and download link, along with standard navigation and social‑share options.

knowledge_base.title: Education | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The Ranchi district education page presents an overview of the school infrastructure and enrolment statistics for the district’s blocks.  

It begins with a brief statement about the Jharkhand government’s commitment to delivering quality education, especially in remote rural areas.  

The main content consists of two tables:

1. **School‑type distribution by block** – For each of the 18 blocks (Angara, Bero, Itki, Bundu, Burmu, Khelari, Chanho, Kanke, Lapung, Mandar, Namkum, Ormanjhi, Ratu, Nagri, Silli, Sonhatu, Tamar, Rahe) the table lists how many schools fall into each category:  
   * Primary only  
   * Primary with Upper Primary  
   * Primary with Upper Primary and Secondary/Higher Secondary  
   * Upper Primary only  
   * Upper Primary with Secondary/Higher Secondary  
   * Total number of schools  
   * Number of Local Body or Private schools  

   The final row provides the district‑wide totals for each column.

2. **Student enrolment by block** – A second table shows, for the same set of blocks (where data are available), the number of students enrolled in classes I‑V, classes VI‑VIII, and the combined total for those two groups.  

Together, these tables give citizens a clear picture of how many schools of each type exist in every block and how many children are attending the primary and upper‑primary levels across Ranchi district. The information is useful for understanding the reach of government education services and for identifying where additional resources might be needed.

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as a contact directory for disaster‑management services in Ranchi district. It lists the key officials who coordinate emergency response, along with their official email addresses and telephone numbers:

- **District Collector, Ranchi** – email: dc‑ran[at]nic[dot]in, phone: 0651‑2214001  
- **Sub‑Divisional Officer, Ranchi** – email: sdanchi[at]gmail[dot]com, phone: 0651‑2201076  
- **Civil Surgeon, Ranchi** – email: csofficeranchi[at]yahoo[dot]in, phone: 0651‑2215618  

In addition, the page provides an immediate fire‑emergency number: **0651 2283825**. This information enables residents to reach the appropriate authorities quickly during a disaster or fire incident.

knowledge.page_purpose: This page serves as the official information hub for land‑record services in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It explains that citizens can access the state’s land‑record portal ( http://jharbhoomi.nic.in/jhrlrmsmis/ ) to view or obtain details about property records, ownership, and related documents. The page also provides the physical location of the District Revenue Office—Block A, Samaharnalay Bhawan, Ranchi, PIN 834001—and an email address (ac‑ranchi@jharkhandmail.gov.in) for inquiries or assistance concerning land‑record matters. No additional contact numbers or directory listings are presented on the page.

knowledge_base.title: Health | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The Ranchi district health page outlines the government’s health‑care infrastructure in the area. It presents two main sets of information:

1. **Block‑wise health sub‑center counts** – A table shows each primary health centre (PHC) in the district’s blocks (Angara, Bero, Bundu, Burmu, Chanho, Kanke, Lapung, Mandar, Namkum, Ormanjhi, Ratu, Silli, Sonahatu, Tamar) together with the number of sub‑centers attached to it. The totals add up to 328 sub‑centers across all blocks.

2. **Urban health centres** – A second table lists the government‑run urban health facilities located throughout Ranchi city, naming specific localities such as Karam Toli Chowk, Karbla Chowk Bahu Bazar Road, Tupudana Chowk, Ward Office – Nayak Mohalla, T.V. Tower – Ratu Road, and many others, covering a total of 22 sites.

Together, these tables give citizens a clear view of where rural sub‑centers are distributed by block and where urban health service points are situated in the city, reflecting the district’s effort to provide accessible health care across both rural and urban areas.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the Page**  
This notice informs the public about the preliminary steps being taken for land acquisition related to the construction of the Karanji Reservoir project in Ranchi district.

**Key Information Provided**  

- **Project:** Construction of the Karanji Reservoir in Village Karanji, Thana Bero (Thana No‑49), Block Bero, District Ranchi.  
- **Land Details:** The acquisition involves plots totaling 0.17 acres (approximately 0.068 hectares) measured in standard units.  
- **Authority:** The action follows approval from the Deputy Commissioner, Ranchi, and references preliminary notification No‑52 dated 16 February 2026.  
- **Timeline:** The notification is open for public view from **18 February 2026** to **18 March 2026**.  
- **Document:** A PDF file (≈1 MB) containing the approved notification format is available for download or viewing via the link:  
  https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/s32b8a61594b1f4c4db0902a8a395ced93/uploads/2026/02/17713930942006.pdf  

Citizens can review the attached PDF to understand the exact land parcels involved and the procedural details of the acquisition process. The notice also includes standard social‑media sharing options for wider dissemination.

knowledge_base.title: Tagore Hill | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an official tourism guide for Tagore Hill in Ranchi, Jharkhand. It introduces the hill as a scenic spot in the Morabadi locality, noting its historical background as the former ashram of Rabindranath Tagore’s elder brother and earlier as a rest house. The description highlights the panoramic sunrise and sunset views from the summit, the presence of the Ramkrishna Mission Ashram at the base, and the hill’s appeal to sightseers, adventure enthusiasts, and rock climbers.

Practical visitor information is provided under a “How to Reach” section, which outlines access by air (mentioning Ranchi Airport) and indicates that the site can be reached locally by rickshaws, taxis, or auto‑rickshaws from anywhere in the city. A photo gallery showcases images of the hill, with links to view additional pictures. The page also includes standard navigation aids such as a breadcrumb trail, social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter), and a direction button that opens Google Maps with the hill’s coordinates. Overall, the content aims to inform citizens and tourists about the hill’s attractions, historical context, and how to get there.

knowledge_base.title: Map of District | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “Map of District” page on the Ranchi district website serves as a quick reference for citizens to locate the administrative offices of the various blocks and subdivisions within Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It combines a visual district map with clickable links that open each block office’s location on Google Maps, making it easy for residents to find directions, contact points, or nearby government facilities.

**Key content presented**

- **Subdivision information** – The page highlights the Ranchi subdivision and lists all the blocks that fall under it.  
- **Block listings** – Each block is presented with a sequential number and a direct Google Maps link to its block/CO office. The blocks mentioned include:  
  - Kanke, Ratu, Chanho, Mandar, Lapung, Rahe, Bundu, Sonahatu, Tamar, Burmu, Bero, Namkum, Nagri, Ormanjhi, Itki, Angara, Silli, Khelari, and others (as shown in the structured items and tables).  
- **Interactive map images** – Two images are embedded:  
  1. A general map of Ranchi district.  
  2. A “Location Index Map” that likely indexes the blocks for quick visual reference.  
- **Navigation aids** – Breadcrumb trail (Home → Map of District) and social‑share buttons (Facebook, X) are provided for easy sharing of the page.

**What a citizen can do**  
By clicking any block’s link, the user is taken to Google Maps showing the exact coordinates of that block’s office, enabling them to get driving directions, view nearby landmarks, or plan a visit to the relevant government office. The page thus functions as a practical directory‑style map tool for accessing Ranchi’s block‑level administration.

knowledge_base.title: Document | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: This page serves as the document repository for the Ranchi district administration. It provides a filter dropdown that lets users narrow down documents by category—currently offering options to view all documents or specifically those related to the 2011 Census data. At the moment, the repository does not display any documents under the selected filter, indicating that either no files are available in that category or the list is empty. The layout includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, social‑share buttons) and a search/filter interface to help citizens locate official documents when they are added.

knowledge_base.title: Past Events | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The “Past Events” page on the Ranchi district website serves as a record of notable activities and initiatives that have taken place in the district. It presents each event as a clickable card that includes the event title, the dates it was held, a brief description, and a link to a detailed page where more information can be found.

The page currently highlights three events:

1. **IT Conclave 2018, Ranchi** – Held on 25 February 2018 at Dhruwa Ground. The event focused on information‑technology discussions and networking. A “View Details” button leads to a dedicated page with further information, and social‑share options allow users to share the event on Facebook or X (formerly Twitter).

2. **COVID‑19 Vaccine FAQ Guidelines** – Available from 16 January 2021 through 31 March 2021. This event provided frequently‑asked‑questions and guidance related to the COVID‑19 vaccination campaign. A PDF document (approximately 2 MB) is attached for download, and the event can be shared via the same social‑media buttons.

3. **Gram Swaraj Abhiyan** – Conducted from 14 April 2018 to 15 August 2018. This initiative aimed at promoting rural self‑governance and development. The entry includes a link to a detailed page and the standard sharing options.

In addition to the event listings, the page provides two downloadable assets:
- A 2 MB PDF related to the COVID‑19 vaccine FAQ guidelines (uploaded January 2021).
- A 1 MB PDF from June 2018, likely supporting material for one of the earlier events.

Overall, the page offers citizens a clear, organized view of recent district‑level programs, complete with dates, brief descriptions, and direct access to supplementary documents or further details.

knowledge.page_purpose: The page is a photo‑gallery hosted by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand) that showcases the waterfalls found in and around Ranchi. The gallery includes navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, share buttons for Facebook and X, and view‑switcher icons) to help visitors browse the images easily. knowledge_base.title: About District | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “About District” page serves as an informational overview for citizens and visitors, presenting a concise profile of Ranchi district—the capital of Jharkhand—so that readers can quickly grasp its administrative structure, key attributes, and notable features.

**Key content highlighted on the page**  

- **Administrative layout** – Ranchi district is divided into two subdivisions: Ranchi and Bundu. The Ranchi subdivision comprises 14 blocks, while the Bundu subdivision comprises 4 blocks, giving a total of 18 blocks and 305 panchayats across the district.  
- **Geographic and climatic notes** – Ranchi is described as having a moderate climate and having served as the summer capital of Bihar until Jharkhand was formed in 2000. It is popularly referred to as the “City of Water Falls.”  
- **Educational hub** – The city attracts students from Bihar, Jharkhand and parts of West Bengal for senior secondary education and hosts several prestigious institutions, including BIT Mesra, IIM, NIFFT, NUSRL, CIP and RIMS.  
- **Government and industrial presence** – Ranchi houses the head offices of important government organisations such as HEC, CCL and SAIL.  
- **Sports facilities** – The district boasts quality sports infrastructure with multiple international‑level stadiums for various sports.  
- **Tourist attractions** – Nature enthusiasts can find numerous waterfalls, hills and forests within a 70‑100 km radius of the city.  

Overall, the page provides a citizen‑friendly snapshot of Ranchi’s administrative divisions, climate, educational and governmental significance, recreational amenities, and nearby natural sights.

knowledge_base.title: Information regarding admission to 25% reserved Seats in initial classes of recognized private school under RTE Act for students of BPL families. | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This notice from the Ranchi district administration informs citizens about the admission process for the 25 % of seats reserved in recognized private schools under the Right‑to‑Education (RTE) Act for children belonging to Below‑Poverty‑Line (BPL) families.  

The notice provides the essential details of the admission window:

- **Purpose:** To announce the schedule and procedure for applying to the reserved seats in private schools for the upcoming academic session.  
- **Key Information:**  
  - **Title/Description:** “Information regarding admission to 25 % reserved Seats in initial classes of recognized private school under RTE Act for students of BPL families.”  
  - **Application Period:** Starts on **14 February 2026** and ends on **10 April 2026**.  
  - **Supporting Document:** A PDF file (approximately 2 MB) that contains the full guidelines, eligibility criteria, and application forms. The file can be viewed or downloaded via the link provided in the notice.  

Citizens interested in securing a seat for their BPL‑eligible child should review the attached PDF for complete instructions and submit their application within the specified dates. The notice also includes standard social‑media sharing options for spreading the information.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
This page serves as an archive of past recruitment notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It allows citizens to view earlier job‑related announcements, results, counselling schedules and related documents that have been published on the official website.

**Key content you will find**

- **List of notices** – Each entry shows a Hindi title, a short description, the start and end dates for the notice, and a link to the downloadable PDF file.  
- **Types of recruitment covered** – The archive includes notices for:
  - Results of the National Health Mission (NHM) advertisement 1/2025.  
  - Counselling processes for selected assistant teachers (inter‑trained 1‑5 and graduate‑trained 6‑8).  
  - Various other recruitment processes (e.g., BPL‑related RTE notices, teacher‑training posts, and miscellaneous district‑level vacancies).  
- **Important dates** – For each notice the page displays the date the notice was opened (“प्रारंभ तिथि”) and the closing date (“अंतिम तिथि”), enabling users to see when the opportunity was active.  
- **Downloadable documents** – Every notice provides a PDF link (often labelled “देखें” with the file size, e.g., 1 MB, 146 KB, 347 KB, etc.) that contains the full notice, press releases, office orders, candidate lists, or counselling slips.  
- **Additional files** – In some cases the table lists supplementary attachments such as a “Candidate List”, “Check Slip New 2016”, or multiple PDFs linked together, giving users access to all related materials in one place.

Overall, the page is a straightforward, date‑ordered repository that lets residents of Ranchi review past recruitment announcements, verify results, and obtain the official documents associated with each notice.

राँची जिला हेतु विज्ञापन पत्र संख्या-415दिनांक-19.11.2025 के तहत योग्य एवंअयोग्य आवेदकों की सूची का प्रकाशन।योग्य आवेदकों के लिए शारीरिक परीक्षण एवंसाक्षात्कार दिनांक-16.12.2025 के पूर्वाह्न08ः30 बजे से बिरसा मुण्डा फुटबॉल स्टेडियम,मोराबादी, राँची में निर्धारित है।नोट- योग्य आवेदक अपने साथ सभी मूलदस्तावेज सहित शारीरिक परीक्षण एवंसाक्षात्कार में उपस्थित होना अनिवार्य है।
गृह रक्षक नवनामांकन हेतु शारीरिक जाँच एवं हिन्दी लेखन परीक्षा की सूचना-जिला राँची।

knowledge_base.title: 3-Tier Panchayat(General) Election 2022 | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page “3‑Tier Panchayat (General) Election 2022” on the Ranchi district website serves as the official information hub for the panchayat elections held in Ranchi, Jharkhand in 2022.  

**What the page provides**

- **Observer details** – A table lists the observers appointed for the election, showing their serial number, name, designation (General Observer or Expenditure Observer), the sub‑division they were assigned to (Sadar or Bundu), the name of their liaison officer, and a contact mobile number. This lets citizens know who was overseeing the poll process in each area and how to reach them.

- **Official notifications and letters** – Links to a series of PDF documents released by the district administration, including:
  * Notification Letter No‑1152(ii) dated 15‑06‑2022  
  * Letter No 1083(ii) dated 04‑06‑2022 (amendment notice)  
  * Letter No 1046(ii) dated 28‑05‑2022 (election notice)  
  * Notification regarding a change in observer  
  * Press note on the Panchayat Election 2022  
  * Several numeric notifications (Nos. 729, 541, etc.) dated April 2022  
  * Election Symbol Allotment Order  

  These files contain the formal orders, schedules, and procedural instructions that governed the election.

- **Polling Duty App** – A prominent button and image direct users to a Google‑Drive link where the “Polling Duty” Android application can be downloaded. The app was likely used by polling staff for duty assignments and real‑time updates.

- **Visuals and navigation** – The page includes a banner image, a photograph related to the panchayat election, and standard website elements (breadcrumb navigation, social‑share buttons for Facebook and X) to help visitors locate the content and share it.

Overall, the page consolidates all essential election‑related information—observer assignments, official directives, press communications, and a useful mobile tool—into a single, accessible resource for citizens, candidates, and election officials in Ranchi district.

knowledge_base.title: DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | CITY OF FALLS | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The Ranchi district website serves as an informational portal for citizens, offering a quick overview of the district’s geography, administration, and notable features.  

At the top of the page a rotating banner carousel displays several images; one of the slides links to a MyGov subscription page (https://cbpssubscriber.mygov.in/aff/fr9Ap8608OHWeScK), suggesting a promotional or outreach element.  

Below the carousel, the “About District” section provides a concise description of Ranchi:

- Ranchi is the capital of Jharkhand and is split into two subdivisions—Ranchi and Bundu—each further divided into blocks, panchayats, and villages.  
- The district comprises 18 blocks in total (14 under Ranchi Sub‑Division and 4 under Bundu Sub‑Division) and 305 panchayats.  
- Historically, Ranchi served as the summer capital of Bihar until Jharkhand was created in 2000.  
- The city enjoys a moderate climate and is popularly known as the “City of Water Falls.”  

A “More About District” link invites visitors to explore additional details. Overall, the page aims to inform residents and visitors about the district’s administrative layout, climatic attributes, and cultural identity, while also highlighting a current government‑linked campaign via the banner carousel.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page is the “Past Notices → Announcements” section of the Ranchi district website. It serves as a public archive of recent government notices, allowing citizens to view and download official announcements that have been issued over the past several months.

Each entry in the table lists:

* **Title** – a brief headline of the notice (e.g., pension applications for players and coaches, road‑project publications, home‑guard enrollment extensions, teacher seniority list, vehicle‑hire tender, medicine supply tender, etc.).
* **Description** – a short explanation of what the notice covers, often referencing the relevant policy, project details, or action required.
* **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which the notice was open for action or viewing (e.g., the pension notice ran from 13 Aug 2025 to 18 Aug 2025; the home‑guard enrollment extension was from 08 Jul 2025 to 16 Jul 2025).
* **File** – a downloadable PDF (with file size indicated) that contains the full notice or supporting document. Links point to files hosted on the government’s CDN, such as the pension notice (1 MB), the Section‑19 road project notice (4 MB), the Section‑11 road‑construction notice (2 MB), and others ranging from a few hundred kilobytes to several megabytes.

Overall, the page consolidates a variety of administrative updates—sports‑pension schemes, infrastructure projects, recruitment extensions, educational staffing, procurement tenders, and public‑health supplies—providing citizens with a single place to review what the Ranchi district administration has recently announced and to access the official documents for further details or action.

knowledge_base.title: How to Reach | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose:**  
The page serves as a practical guide for residents and visitors on how to travel to Ranchi, the district headquarters of Jharkhand, by outlining the main modes of transport available.

**Key Content:**  

- **By Air:** Mentions that the nearest domestic airport is Ranchi Airport, providing the primary air‑travel option for reaching the city.  
- **By Rail:** Notes that Ranchi Railway Division covers a total track length of 842 km, with several rail routes converging at the junction, making Ranchi an important transit hub in eastern India.  
- **By Road:** Highlights that three major National Highways—NH 7 (North‑South), NH 47 (West‑bound), and NH 68 (East‑bound)—intersect in Ranchi. It also lists the three principal bus stands that facilitate intra‑ and inter‑city travel: ITI Bus Stand, Khadghara Bus Stand, and Government Bus Stand.  

The page also includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, home and tourism links) and social‑share options for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter), allowing users to easily share the information. Overall, it offers a clear, citizen‑focused overview of the transport infrastructure that connects Ranchi to other parts of the country.

knowledge_base.title: Tenders |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page is the “Tenders” section of the District Ranchi website (Government of Jharkhand). It displays current procurement opportunities that the district administration is inviting bids for. Three tenders are shown in a table, each with a title, brief description, bidding period, and a link to the detailed PDF notice.

1. **Short Term Tender Notice – Expression of Interest (EOI)**  
   *Purpose:* Invites suppliers, agencies, firms or establishments to submit expressions of interest for supplying food items as per a prescribed menu to players at various residential training centres operating in Ranchi district.  
   *Bidding window:* Starts 19 March 2026 and ends 4 April 2026.  
   *Document:* PDF (≈ 9 MB) available for download.

2. **BOOK TENDER – MPLAD**  
   *Purpose:* Seeks bids for the supply of books to be funded under the Member of Parliament Local Area Development Scheme (MPLAD).  
   *Bidding window:* Starts 14 March 2026 and ends 27 March 2026.  
   *Document:* PDF (≈ 823 KB) available for download.

3. **Tender Invitation – Upgradation of Education Facilities**  
   *Purpose:* Invites proposals for upgrading education facilities in government schools of Ranchi district, including the installation of ICT labs, libraries, and Recreation‑cum‑Learning Centres.  
   *Bidding window:* Starts 9 March 2026 and ends 27 March 2026.  
   *Document:* PDF (≈ 8 MB) available for download.

For each tender, the page provides a “View” link that opens the full notice (PDF) in a new window, allowing interested parties to review eligibility criteria, submission requirements, and other details. An “Archive” button at the bottom lets users access past tender notices. This page serves as a central, up‑to‑date source for businesses and service providers looking to participate in district‑level government procurement.

knowledge_base.title: हुन्डरु जलप्रपात | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as an online photo gallery for Hundru Falls (हुन्डरु जलप्रपात), a well‑known waterfall located in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It is maintained by the District Administration of Ranchi to showcase the natural beauty of the site and to make visual information easily accessible to residents and visitors.

**Key content presented**  

- **Title and navigation** – The page header displays the gallery’s name “हुन्डरु जलप्रपात” and a breadcrumb trail that links back to the Ranchi district homepage and the main photo‑galleries section, helping users understand where they are within the site.  

- **Main image** – A single, high‑resolution photograph of Hundru Falls is featured. The image is hosted on the government’s CDN (https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/…) and can be viewed in full size by clicking on it. A thumbnail version is also shown in the gallery layout.  

- **View‑switcher controls** – Icons allow users to toggle between a grid view and a list view of the gallery items, providing flexibility in how the photos are browsed.  

- **Social sharing tools** – Buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) enable visitors to share the gallery page directly from the site, with pre‑filled text that includes the page URL and the gallery title in Hindi.  

- **Caption and description** – Beneath the image, the caption “हुन्डरु जलप्रपात” identifies the subject, reinforcing the gallery’s focus on this waterfall.  

Overall, the page provides citizens with a simple, visually oriented way to explore and share images of Hundru Falls, supporting tourism promotion and local pride in the district’s natural heritage.

A direct link to the national Parivahan portal (https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan/) is also given for citizens to access transport‑related services online. The page serves as a guide for residents seeking contact details and location of the local transport authority.

knowledge_base.title: RTI | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as the Ranchi district’s information hub for the Right to Information (RTI) Act. It explains that RTI is a parliamentary law granting citizens a practical regime to access government information, replacing the earlier Freedom of Information Act, 2002.  

Key resources provided on the page include:

- A link to the **official RTI website** (rtionline.gov.in) for general information and guidance.  
- A downloadable **PIO and APIO List for Ranchi** (PDF, 332 KB) that details the Public Information Officers and Assistant Public Information Officers responsible for handling RTI requests in the district.  
- A link to submit an **online RTI request** directly through the RTI online portal.  
- A **User Manual for RTI** (PDF) that outlines the procedural steps citizens should follow when filing an RTI application.  

Together, these elements give residents of Ranchi clear pathways to learn about their RTI rights, identify the appropriate officers to contact, and file requests either online or via the provided documents.
