knowledge_base.title: Contact Details | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as a public directory of contact information for officials in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It enables citizens to reach the appropriate government officers for inquiries, complaints, or assistance related to various district‑level services.

**Key content**  

- **District‑level officers** – A table lists senior officials such as the Deputy Commissioner‑cum‑District Magistrate and Collector, Deputy Development Commissioner, Superintendent of Police (S.S.P.), District Agriculture Officer, Civil Surgeon, Additional Collector, Sub‑Divisional Officers (Sadar and Bundu), District Transport Officer, and District Panchayati Raj Officer. For each officer the page provides an official e‑mail address and a telephone number (e.g., the DC’s phone 0651‑2214001, the S.S.P.’s mobile 9431706136, the Civil Surgeon’s mobile 7544052143, etc.).

- **Additional district contacts** – The same table continues with other district posts such as Nazarat Deputy Collector, AC Naxal, Director DRDA, District Land Acquisition Officer, Director Rashtriya Niyojan Karyakram, ADM Law & Order, Deputy Collector Land Reforms, and District Commandant Ranchi (including CTI Dhurwa). Where available, e‑mail addresses and phone numbers are shown.

- **Block Development Officers** – A second table provides contact details for the Block Development Officers of the six blocks in Ranchi district (Bero, Bundu, Burmu, Angara, Itki, and Kanke). Each entry includes an e‑mail address (written in a “[at] … [dot]” format to avoid spam) and a mobile phone number (e.g., BDO Bero 7004182552, BDO Bundu 8810520995, etc.).

Overall, the page consolidates essential contact points—e‑mail IDs and phone numbers—for both district‑level and block‑level government functionaries, making it easier for residents to locate and communicate with the relevant authorities.

- **Title and navigation** – The page header reads “टैगोर हिल” and includes a breadcrumb trail (Home → फोटो गैलरी → टैगोर हिल) for easy navigation back to the district portal.

- **View options** – Buttons let users switch between grid and list views of the gallery.  
- **Social sharing** – Icons enable sharing the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) with pre‑filled text and URL.  
- **Description area** – A section for a textual description is present (currently empty).  

Overall, the page serves as a simple, government‑run visual repository for Tagore Hill, providing citizens with easy access to pictures of the landmark and the ability to share them via social media.

knowledge_base.title: Tehsil / Block | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an informational guide to the revenue subdivisions (tehsils/blocks) of Ranchi district in Jharkhand. It explains that district‑level revenue administration is headed by the Deputy Commissioner (who also acts as the District Collector), with an Additional Collector, a District Collector Land Records (DCLR) officer at the sub‑division level, and Circle Officers assisted by Circle Inspectors and revenue staff (Karamcharis) at the block level.

The main content is a list of the twenty‑two circles (blocks) that make up Ranchi district. For many of the blocks the page provides a direct Google‑Maps link to the block office location, allowing citizens to view the exact address and get directions. The blocks shown with map links include Angara, Bero, Burmu, Chanho, Itki, Khelari, Kanke, Lapung, Mandar, Nagri, Namkum, Ormanjhi and Rahe. The remaining blocks (Bundu and a few others) are listed by name without a map link.

In addition to the block list, the page contains standard site navigation aids—a breadcrumb trail showing the path from the district home page to the “Tehsil / Block” section, and social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) so users can easily share the page. A banner image and the usual header/footer elements of the district website are also present. Overall, the page helps residents locate and identify the administrative offices responsible for revenue matters in each block of Ranchi.

knowledge_base.title: Gram Swaraj Abhiyan | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Gram Swaraj Abhiyan – Ranchi District (Jharkhand)**  

This page announces the “Gram Swaraj Abhiyan” campaign organized by the Ranchi district administration. The initiative was launched on the occasion of Ambedkar Jayanti and ran from 14 April 2018 to 15 August 2018, with activities centred at the DC Office and all block offices across the district.

**Purpose**  
The campaign aimed to:

* Foster social harmony and spread awareness of the government’s pro‑poor schemes.  
* Reach out to disadvantaged households to enroll them in welfare programmes and collect their feedback on those initiatives.  
* Achieve saturation of eligible beneficiaries under seven flagship programmes in 21,058 identified villages.

**Key Programmes Covered**  
The saturation drive focused on the following central schemes:

* Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana  
* Saubhagya (electricity for all)  
* Ujala scheme (LED distribution)  
* Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana  
* Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana  
* Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana  
* Mission Indradhanush (immunisation)

**Additional Resource**  
A downloadable PDF (≈1 MB) provides further details about the Abhiyan; it can be accessed via the “View” button on the page.

In short, the page informs citizens about a district‑level outreach effort designed to ensure that poor households are aware of, enrolled in, and can evaluate major government welfare programmes.

knowledge_base.title: प्रमाण पत्र | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose: This page serves as the official information hub for obtaining various certificates (प्रमाण पत्र) from the Ranchi district administration in Jharkhand. It directs citizens to the Jharkhand e‑Seva portal (http://www.jharsewa.jharkhand.gov.in/) where they can apply for or track certificate services online. Additionally, the page lists the contact details of the Director of the District Rural Development Agency (DRDA), including the office address at Vikash Bhavan, Kachahri Road, Ranchi (PIN 834001) and the email address helpdesk[dot]jharsewa[at]gmail[dot]com for assistance. The content is focused on guiding users to the appropriate online service point and providing a direct government contact for certificate‑related inquiries.

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as a directory of higher‑education institutions in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It presents a list of colleges and universities, each entry giving the institution’s name, its physical address (including locality, pincode and, where available, a Google‑Maps link), and a direct link to the institution’s official website when provided.  

Key details included for the listed entities are:

- **IIM Ranchi** – address at Suchna Bhawan, 5th floor, Audrey House Campus, Meer Road, Ranchi, Jharkhand 834008; website http://www.iimranchi.ac.in.  
- **Central University, Jharkhand** – located on Ratu‑Lohardaga Road, Brambe, Ranchi 835 205; a map link is supplied.  
- **Other institutions** – such as the engineering college referenced by the link to BIT Mesra (https://www.bitmesra.ac.in/), the college linked to http://bauranchi.org, and the women’s college linked to http://www.ranchiwomenscollege.org/, each with its respective address and pincode.  

The directory is organized in a simple list format, allowing citizens to quickly locate contact information, location details, and web resources for the colleges and universities operating within Ranchi district.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is the “Past Notices → Tenders” section for Ranchi district (Government of Jharkhand). It serves as a public archive of completed tender notices, allowing citizens to see what procurement opportunities have been announced and when they were open for bidding.

Each entry in the table shows:

* **Title** – a brief name of the tender (e.g., “Tender for the supply of classical and patent medicines in the financial year 2025‑26”, “VERY SHORT TENDER – hiring of four‑wheelers”, “Book Supply”, “HalfWay Homes”, or tenders related to the Birsa Harit Gram Yojna for fruit‑plant and material supply).  
* **Description** – a short explanation of what is being procured or the purpose of the tender (such as supplying medicines to Ayurvedic centres, renting Bolero vehicles for the Sidhkofed office, providing books on MLA recommendation, establishing halfway homes, or supplying mango saplings and other materials under the Birsa Harit Gram Yojna).  
* **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which the tender was open for submission (all dates are in DD/MM/YYYY format, ranging from late March 2025 to mid‑July 2025).  
* **File** – a link to the PDF notice (with file size indicated, e.g., “View (4 MB)”, “View (325 KB)”, etc.) that can be downloaded or viewed in a new window.

Overall, the page provides a transparent record of recent government procurement activities in Ranchi, giving residents easy access to the details of each tender, the timeframe it was active, and the official documents for reference.

It lists several major banks—including IDBI Bank, ICICI Bank, IndusInd Bank, HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Jharkhand Gramin Bank, Dena Bank, Federal Bank, Bandhan Bank, and Bank of India—each presented with a brief heading and two key details:

1. **Location link** – a Google‑Maps search URL that points to the bank’s branch or office in Ranchi (e.g., “IDBI Bank – Kataruka House, Ranchi”, “ICICI Bank – Ratu Road, Ranchi”, etc.). Clicking the link opens a map view of the specified address.

2. **Email address** – a contact e‑mail for the branch, shown in a format that replaces “@” and “.” with words (e.g., `somenath_kundu[at]idbi[dot]co[dot]in`). This provides a direct way for citizens to reach the bank’s local office for inquiries or services.

The page is organized as a simple list, with each bank entry displayed in a boxed section that includes the bank’s name, the map link, and the e‑mail contact. It helps residents quickly locate nearby bank branches and obtain a point of contact for banking‑related needs.

knowledge_base.title: e-Nibandhan | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page describes the **e‑Nibandhan** service offered by the District Land Registry Office in Ranchi, Jharkhand. It explains that e‑Nibandhan is an online facility for land‑related registration and provides a direct link to the official portal where citizens can access the service: **http://regd.jharkhand.gov.in/jars/website/**.  

In addition to the online link, the page lists the physical address of the District Land Registry Office for those who need to visit in person:

- **Location:** Katuchery Chowk (opposite RRDA Office), Collectorate Office, Ranchi  
- **PIN Code:** 834001  

The content is presented in a straightforward layout, with a banner image, breadcrumb navigation showing the path Home → Services → e‑Nibandhan, and social‑share options for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter). The purpose of the page is to inform citizens about how to use the e‑Nibandhan service—both online and at the office—so they can complete land‑registry transactions conveniently.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The Ranchi district “Past Notices → Announcements” page serves as a public notice board where the district administration posts recent official communications for citizens. It currently features a table of announcements that includes:

- **Counseling process for 16 selected candidates** for Graduate Trained Teacher (classes 6‑8) and Inter‑trained Teacher (classes 1‑5), with counseling scheduled from 27 December 2025 to 29 December 2025 and links to the notice, candidate list and check‑slip PDFs.  
- **Book tender for the State Library, Ranchi**, inviting suppliers to provide books and study materials; the tender is open from 17 December 2025 to 24 December 2025, with the tender notice and a library‑book list available for download.  
- **Advertisement for the District Level Youth Festival‑2025**, announcing competitions (folk dance, folk song, poetry, painting, declamation) to be held on 20 December 2025 at Audrey House, Morabadi; the notice runs from 13 December 2025 to 23 December 2025 and includes a Hindi‑language information sheet.  
- **Reference to Advertisement No. 415/2025** for the Day‑Boarding Coach post, publishing the eligible/not‑eligible applicant list and scheduling the physical test and interview for 16 December 2025 at Birsa Munda Football Stadium; the notice is valid from 12 December 2025 to 20 December 2025.  
- **Information on private schools that have applied for Right‑to‑Education (RTE) recognition**, provided as a downloadable PDF.  
- **Tender notices** for food and daily‑use items for the Deaf & Dumb School, and for the purchase of blankets, each with its own PDF attachment.  
- **Details on the distribution of appointment letters** for newly appointed Sahayak Aacharya (Inter‑trained and Graduate‑trained) in Ranchi district.  
- **Advertisement for sports trainers** to fill vacant positions in managed/un‑managed day‑boarding sports training centers under the Tourism, Art‑Culture, Sports and Youth Affairs Department, with an application deadline of 26 November 2025.  
- **Supply of books & study material** under the State‑level Ankansha Programme, with a submission deadline of 24 November 2025 at 04:00 PM.

Each entry includes a clear title, a brief description, the start and end dates during which the notice is relevant, and one or more downloadable PDF files (often the main notice plus supporting lists or forms). Citizens can browse this page to stay informed about recruitment processes, tender opportunities, educational initiatives, youth events, and other district‑level announcements, and directly access the official documents for further details or to apply.

knowledge_base.title: Economy | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “Economy” section of the Ranchi district website is intended to give residents, investors, and other stakeholders a clear picture of the district’s economic landscape. It highlights why Ranchi is an attractive place for business and livelihood, outlines the sectors driving growth, and points out the natural advantages that support various industries.

**Key content covered**

- **Overall economic situation** – Ranchi, as the state capital, is experiencing fast‑paced urbanization and infrastructure expansion. The availability of skilled manpower, reputable technical and educational institutions, good transport and communication networks, and improving power supply make the Ranchi Industrial Area (RIA) a draw for entrepreneurs. The page stresses the need for sustainable, eco‑friendly development alongside industrial growth.

- **Trade, commerce and business** – Ranchi serves as a major hub for trade and commerce in Jharkhand. A high literacy rate, a hardworking workforce, political stability, and basic facilities create a favorable environment for businesses of all sizes—from neighborhood shops and medical stores to branded retail outlets, fast‑food chains, multiplexes, and malls. The district hosts multinational corporations, public‑sector enterprises, and private limited companies, offering employment opportunities for professionals and encouraging new ventures through government‑run incentive schemes.

- **Mineral and natural resources** – The district’s surrounding forested area supplies a range of raw materials used by industries such as construction, furniture, match‑box manufacturing, paper, rayon, railway sleepers, and wooden poles. Forest produce is divided into:
  - **Major products** – timber varieties including bamboo, mahua, shisham, kusum, aam (mango), jamun, saal, imli (tamarind), gamhar, etc.
  - **Minor products** – items such as hara, behara, kendu patta, saal seed, karanj seed, mahua patta, etc., many of which have medicinal or commercial uses.

Together, these sections inform citizens about the district’s economic strengths, the opportunities available for employment and entrepreneurship, and the natural assets that underpin local industry.

knowledge_base.title: PINCODE RANCHI DISTRICT | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as an official reference for the postal (PIN) codes used by various offices and taluks within Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It is maintained by the District Administration to help residents, businesses, and government agencies locate the correct PIN code for mail delivery and related services.

**Key content**  
- A clearly labelled table lists each entry with a serial number, the name of the **Office**, its associated **Taluk**, the **District** (Ranchi), the **State** (Jharkhand), and the corresponding **Pincode**.  
- The table covers a wide range of localities across the district, including offices in taluks such as Bundu, Mander, Burmu, Tamar, Angara, Khunti, Silli, Bhandra, Itki, Murhu, Mandar, and others.  
- Sample entries from the table include:  
  - Chama (Bundu taluk) – PIN 829205  
  - Dakra Colliery (Dakra taluk) – PIN 829210  
  - Diwan Nagar (Mander taluk) – PIN 829205  
  - Hindgir (Mander taluk) – PIN 829209  
  - Khalari (Mander taluk) – PIN 829205  
  - Mc. Cluskiganj (Mander taluk) – PIN 829208  
  - Nawadih (Burmu taluk) – PIN 829208  
  - Nindra (Mander taluk) – PIN 829208  
  - Ray (Bundu taluk) – PIN 829209  
  - Adalahatu (Bundu taluk) – PIN 835204  
  - Adalhatu (Ranchi taluk) – PIN 834008  
  - AG Bihar (Ranchi taluk) – PIN 834002  
  - …and many more, continuing through additional offices such as Angara, Tamar, Khunti, Silli, Bhandra, Itki, Murhu, and Mandar.  

The information is presented in a straightforward, tabular format that allows citizens to quickly scan for the office or taluk they need and obtain the correct PIN code for postal correspondence. This resource is particularly useful for filling out forms, sending mail, or verifying address details when interacting with government services in Ranchi district.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “कौन कौन है?” (Who’s Who) section of the Ranchi district website serves as a directory for citizens to find contact details of the senior officials who administer the district. It lets residents quickly locate the right person to approach for administrative matters, grievances, or information.

**Key content**  
The page presents a table titled **“जिला स्तरीय अधिकारी” (District‑level Officers)** that lists two senior posts:

| Position | Email | Address | Phone | Fax |
|----------|-------|---------|-------|-----|
| **प्रमंडलीय आयुक्त** (Commissioner, South Chotanagpur Division) | scnd[dot]comm[dot]ranc[at]gmail[dot]com | दक्षिणी छोटानागपुर प्रमंडल रांची | 0651‑2214610 | (blank) |
| **उपायुक्त, राँची (नज़ारत)** (Deputy Commissioner, Ranchi – Nazarat) | dc-ran[at]nic[dot]in | रांची कलेक्ट्रेट रांची, 302, डीसी ऑफिस रांची, कचहरी चौक, रांची, झारखंड, 834001 | 0651‑2214001 | 91‑651‑2360120 |

The table includes the official’s designation, official email address (presented in a click‑to‑copy format), full postal address, telephone number, and, where available, a fax number. A filter dropdown at the top allows users to view all entries or limit the display to “जिला स्तरीय अधिकारी” (district‑level officers). This structure makes it easy for residents to locate the appropriate contact for district‑level governance.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page explains how the Ranchi district administration is organized and who carries out the various governmental functions in the area. It is intended to help citizens understand the structure of local government and the responsibilities of the key officials.

**Key content**  

- **Role of the Deputy Commissioner** – The Deputy Commissioner is the chief officer of the district administration. In addition to this post, the same officer acts as:  
  * Collector (revenue matters)  
  * District Development Commissioner (development works)  
  * District Magistrate (law‑and‑order and general administration)  
  * District Election Officer (election‑related duties)  

- **Support from the Sub‑Divisional Officer** – The Sub‑Divisional Officer assists the Deputy Commissioner in overseeing the sub‑division.

- **Components of the administrative setup** – The page lists the main units that make up the district’s administration:  
  1. **Samaharnalay** (the district collectorate office)  
  2. **Tehsil** (revenue subdivisions)  
  3. **Police** (law‑enforcement machinery)  
  4. **Anumandal & Prakhand** (sub‑divisions and blocks)  
  5. **Gaon & Panchayat** (villages and panchayats)  
  6. **Nirvachan Kshetra** (electoral constituencies)  

Together, these elements describe how governmental services—revenue collection, development planning, policing, local governance, and elections—are administered across Ranchi district. The information is presented in plain Hindi to make it accessible to the local populace.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “Past Notices → Recruitment” section on the Ranchi district website serves as an archive of all recruitment advertisements that have been issued by the district administration. It allows citizens to view, download, and refer back to earlier notices for information about eligibility, schedules, and results.

**Key content displayed**  
The page presents a table of past recruitment notices, each row containing:

| Title (brief description) | Start Date | End Date | Associated PDF files (size & link) |

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as an archive of past recruitment notices issued by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand). It allows citizens to view earlier announcements, download the related PDF documents, and see the time‑frames during which each recruitment process was open.

**Key content**  
The page presents a table of recruitment notices, each row containing:

| Title (brief description of the notice) | Description (more detail) | Start Date | End Date | File (PDF) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Appointment on vacant posts against the posts identified as per ICPS under the Conservation House operated in Ranchi district. | Application form for contractual appointment on vacant posts identified under the Integrated Child Protection Scheme (ICPS) in the Conservation House, Ranchi. | 04/03/2021 | 15/03/2021 | View (1 MB) PDF |
| Bharti Camp 12‑03‑2021 | Details of the Bharti Camp held on 12 March 2021. | 10/03/2021 | 13/03/2021 | View (99 KB) PDF |
| Bharti Camp 12/03/2021 | Information about the Bharti Camp on 12 March 2021. | 10/03/2021 | 12/03/2021 | View (288 KB) PDF |
| Regarding the redressal of the candidates who have applied to various posts of ICPS. | Notice concerning grievance redressal for ICPS applicants. | 22/02/2021 | 04/03/2021 | View (1 MB) PDF |
| Bharti Camp Dtd. 01.02.2021 to 02.02.2021 | Bharti Camp conducted from 1 Feb to 2 Feb 2021. | 28/01/2021 | 03/02/2021 | View (105 KB) PDF |
| RECRUITMENT UNDER INTERGRATED CHILD PROTECTION SCHEME DISTRICT | ICPS 2021 application form for district‑level recruitment. | 26/12/2020 | 15/01/2021 | View (1 MB) PDF (plus an additional 487 KB file) |
| Application Form for Recruitment under Integrated Child Protection Scheme | General ICPS recruitment application form. | 22/12/2020 | 05/01/2021 | View (728 KB) PDF (plus an additional 587 KB file) |
| Recruitment camp | Notice of a recruitment camp. | 24/12/2020 | 28/12/2020 | View (177 KB) PDF |
| Bharti Camp 18‑12‑2020 | Bharti Camp held on 18 December 2020. | 14/12/2020 | 19/12/2020 | (file link present) |
| Bharti‑Camp dtd. 09.12.2020 | Bharti Camp on 9 December 2020. | (dates not shown in the excerpt) | (dates not shown) | (file link present) |

**What a citizen can find**  
- Clear titles that indicate the nature of each recruitment (e.g., ICPS‑related posts, Bharti Camps, redressal notices).  
- Exact start and end dates, showing how long each opportunity was open.  
- Direct download links to PDF files, with file sizes noted (ranging from ~100 KB to ~1 MB), enabling easy access to the full notices or application forms.  
- A breadcrumb trail showing the navigation path: Home →

knowledge_base.title: Rural Development Ranchi | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an informational overview of the Rural Development initiatives being implemented in Ranchi district by the Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. It lists the key flagship programmes currently active in the area, including:

- Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) – wage‑employment scheme  
- DAY‑NRLM – livelihood promotion for rural households  
- Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana‑Gramin – rural housing assistance  
- Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) – construction of quality rural roads  
- National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) – social pension benefits  
- Integrated Watershed Management Programme (IWMP) – land‑productivity improvement  
- Sansad Adarsh Gram Yojana – model village development  
- RuRBAN mission – linking rural and urban economies  

The page also provides a point of contact for the Directorate of Rashtriya Niyojan Karyakram, with a phone number (+91 7004599077), and directs visitors to the national rural development portal (www.rural.nic.in) for further details. This layout helps citizens quickly see what rural‑development schemes are available locally and how to reach the responsible office for inquiries or assistance.

This page serves as the official source for the 2011 Census data specific to Ranchi district in Jharkhand. It allows citizens to view or download the complete census report for the district.

**Key content provided**  

- **Document title:** *Census Data 2011*  
- **Publication / upload date:** 27 April 2018  
- **File size:** Approximately 4 MB  
- **Format:** PDF  
- **Access link:** https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/s32b8a61594b1f4c4db0902a8a395ced93/uploads/2018/04/2018042966.pdf (opens in a new window for viewing or downloading)  

The page also includes standard navigation aids (breadcrumb trail, a filter dropdown to stay within the “Census Data 2011” category, and social‑share buttons for Facebook and X) to help users locate the document easily and share it if desired. No additional tables or listings are present; the focus is solely on providing the census PDF for public access.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an archive of past tender notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. It presents a table of completed procurement opportunities, each entry showing the tender title, a brief description, the period during which bids were invited (start and end dates), and direct links to the associated documents—mostly PDF files hosted on the government’s secure storage service.

The tenders cover a variety of government needs, including:

- A reverse‑auction notice for procurement services.  
- A corrigendum to an earlier tender (Reference No. 210).  
- A two‑year door‑step delivery contract for the years 2020‑21 and 2021‑22, accompanied by supplementary files such as a reverse‑auction notice and an annexure.  
- Expressions of Interest for establishing a model school under an untied fund and for projects under the District Innovation Fund.  
- A quotation request for gardening work at the State Institute of Rural Development (SIRD) in Ranchi.  
- Tender documents related to the 2019 Assembly Election, covering polling material, tents, and computer equipment.  
- An IEC‑BCC (Information, Education, and Communication – Behaviour Change Communication) tender notification.  
- A reserve‑auction procurement notice for purchasing blankets through NEML.  
- A tender for parking‑related works at Sadar Hospital, Ranchi.

Each row provides the exact dates when the tender was open (e.g., 05/03/2020 – 13/03/2020 for the reverse‑auction notice) and a clickable “View” link that displays the file size and opens the PDF in a new window. This layout allows citizens to easily browse completed tenders, verify the timelines, and download the original notices or supporting documents for reference or transparency purposes.

knowledge_base.title: राँची पिक्चर | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is the “राँची पिक्चर” (Ranchi Picture) photo‑gallery section of the official District Ranchi website, maintained by the Government of Jharkhand. Its purpose is to give citizens and visitors a visual overview of notable places and scenes in Ranchi—such as religious sites, landmarks, and other points of interest—by presenting a collection of photographs that can be browsed in grid or list view.

The gallery displays thumbnail images accompanied by short captions (e.g., “सि एन आई चर्च राँची” – CNI Church Ranchi). Clicking a thumbnail opens the full‑size image in a lightbox view, and each item includes social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) so users can easily share the pictures. A breadcrumb trail shows the navigation path (Home → Photo Gallery → राँची पिक्चer), and a view‑switcher lets users toggle between grid and list layouts. The page focuses solely on presenting these images; it does not contain contact details, directories, or tabular data.

knowledge_base.title: Tender Invitation | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is a tender notice published by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. It invites bids for a project to upgrade education facilities in government schools across Ranchi district. The work involves setting up ICT labs, libraries, and Recreation‑cum‑Learning Centres.

Key details from the notice:

- **Title:** Tender Invitation  
- **Description:** Upgradation of education facilities with ICT labs, library, and Recreation Cum Learning Centres in Government Schools of Ranchi District of Jharkhand.  
- **Bid submission period:** Starts on 09 March 2026 and ends on 27 March 2026.  
- **Document:** A PDF file (approximately 8 MB) containing the full tender documents is available for download/viewing via the link provided.

The notice is presented in a simple table format on the website, allowing citizens and potential contractors to view the scope of the project, the timeline for submitting bids, and to access the detailed tender file.

knowledge_base.title: District e-Governance Society (DeGS) | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page introduces the District e‑Governance Society (DeGS) for Ranchi, Jharkhand, explaining that it was set up by the Government of India under the National e‑Governance Plan to strengthen and monitor the Common Service Centre (CSC) project at the district level. It outlines the society’s purpose—providing a coordinated platform for delivering various e‑governance services—and references the government orders that define its roles and responsibilities.

Below the introductory text, the page presents a list of key digital initiatives and portals that DeGS helps promote or oversee. Each item is shown as a clickable card with a brief label and a link to the respective service:

- **CSC e‑Gov** – the national Common Service Centre portal.  
- **JharSewa** – Jharkhand’s citizen‑service gateway.  
- **PMGDISHA** – the Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan for digital literacy.  
- **Digital India** – the overarching national digital empowerment programme.  
- **e‑PDS (Public Distribution System)** – Jharkhand’s online ration‑card and food‑distribution system (accessible via aahar.jharkhand.gov.in).  
- **Jharbhoomi** – the state’s land‑record management portal.  
- **e‑Nibandhan** – the online registration and document‑services platform.  
- **Jharkhand Tender e‑Portal** – the gateway for government tenders in Jharkhand.

A separate table highlights additional important portals that citizens may find useful:

1. **CSC Portal** – main CSC website.  
2. **VLE Registration Portal** – for registering Village Level Entrepreneurs who operate CSCs.  
3. **MEITY** – the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology’s official site.  
4. **DigiPay** – the CSC‑based digital payment service.

Together, these sections give residents of Ranchi a clear view of the e‑governance tools available through DeGS, ranging from service delivery and land records to welfare benefits, digital literacy, and procurement opportunities. The page serves as a citizen‑friendly directory to access these online resources.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the Page**  
The “Past Notices → Recruitment” section of the Ranchi District website serves as an archive of all recruitment‑related announcements that have been issued by the District Administration. It allows citizens to view, download, and refer to official notices, results, counselling schedules, advertisement details, and examination timetables for various government posts that have been advertised in the past.

**Key Content Highlights**  

| Notice / Activity | What It Covers | Important Dates (as shown) | Available Documents |
|-------------------|----------------|----------------------------|----------------------|
| **Result under NHM Adv 1/2025** | Press release, office order, and short‑listed candidate list for the National Health Mission recruitment. | Start 18 Jan 2026 – End 17 Feb 2026 | PDF (≈1 MB) |
| **Counselling Process for 16 Selected Candidates – Graduate Trained Teacher (6‑8) & Inter Trained (1‑5)** | Counselling schedule and candidate list for teacher posts. | Start 27 Dec 2025 – End 29 Dec 2025 | Main notice (≈146 KB), Candidate List (≈347 KB), Check Slip (≈877 KB) |
| **Reference to Advertisement No. 415/2025 – Day‑Boarding Coach** | Lists eligible & ineligible applicants and announces the physical test & interview date. | Start 12 Dec 2025 – End 20 Dec 2025 | PDF (≈1 MB) |
| **Notice for Physical & Written Exam – Home Guard (Adv No. 01/2025)** | Schedule for physical and written examinations for new Home Guard enrolment. | Start 25 Sep 2025 – End 04 Dec 2025 | PDF (≈1 MB) (also mirrored on Google Drive) |
| **Advertisement for Sports Trainers** | Invitation for applications to fill vacant sports‑trainer positions in day‑boarding centres. | Application deadline 26 Nov 2025 (04:00 PM) | PDF (≈4 MB) |
| **Counselling Process for 37 Candidates – Inter Trained (1‑5) & Graduate Trained (6‑8)** | Counselling details for selected teacher candidates. | (Dates embedded in the notice) | PDF (≈6 MB) |
| **Counselling Process for Remaining 32 Candidates – Sahayak Achrya (Lang Subjects, 6‑8)** | Counselling for language‑subject assistant teacher posts. | (Dates embedded in the notice) | PDF (≈4 MB) |
| **Information on RTE Admission for BPL Families** | Guidelines for admitting BPL students in recognized private schools under the Right to Education Act. | (Dates embedded in the notice) | PDF (≈591 KB) |
| **Counselling Process for 302 Selected Candidates – Sahayak Acharya (1‑5)** | Counselling schedule for primary‑level assistant teacher posts. | (Dates embedded in the notice) | PDF (≈? MB) |
| **Distribution of Appointment Letters – Chowkidar Posts** | Issuing appointment letters to finally selected chowkidar candidates from Adv No. 01/2024. | (Dates embedded in the notice) | PDF (≈? MB) |

**What a Citizen Can Do**  
- Browse the table to locate a specific recruitment notice by its title or date range.  
- Click the “View” links to download the corresponding PDF notices, result sheets, counselling schedules, or advertisement documents.  
- Use the information (e.g., exam dates, counselling schedules, application deadlines) to stay informed about past recruitment processes, verify results, or prepare for similar future opportunities.

In summary, the page is a comprehensive, date‑ordered archive of Ranchi District’s recruitment notices, providing easy access to official PDFs that detail results, counselling procedures, examination schedules, and advertisement specifics for a

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The “Past | Announcements” section of the Ranchi district website serves as an archive where citizens can view and download official notices that have been issued by the Government of Jharkhand. Each entry includes a brief title, a description of the notice, the period during which it was active (start and end dates), and a direct link to the corresponding PDF document.

The notices cover a range of topics relevant to residents, including:

* **Tenders** – short‑term tenders for the Sveep Cell, a tender for the District Education Officer, and an invitation to supply and install whiteboards in schools across several blocks.  
* **Recruitment & interviews** – walk‑in interviews for Ayush, Atal MBBS and part‑time UPHC doctors, and information about document verification for vacant MGNREGA positions such as Block Program Officer and Technical Assistants.  
* **Government schemes** – dissemination of FAQs and related material for the Jharkhand Chief Minister’s Maiya Samman Yojana (and its variant Maiyaan Sammaan Yojna).  
* **Education updates** – publication of a provisional list for the promotion of Grade‑7 teachers working in Ranchi’s primary schools.  
* **Election information** – an abstract statement of candidates’ expenses for the 2024 Lok Sabha (General) election.  
* **Community initiatives** – details on the allotment of kiosks for minority communities.

For each notice, the table shows the exact start and end dates (e.g., the Sveep Cell tender ran from 17 Oct 2024 to 23 Oct 2024; the doctor walk‑in interview was held on 16‑17 Oct 2024) and provides a downloadable PDF file (sizes ranging from a few hundred kilobytes to several megabytes). This layout allows citizens to quickly see what announcements were active, when they were applicable, and to access the full documents for further information or action.

knowledge_base.title: जिले के बारे में | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**पृष्ठ का उद्देश्य**  
यह पृष्ठ रांची जिले के बारे में मूलभूत जानकारी प्रदान करता है, ताकि नागरिक जिले की प्रशासनिक संरचना, भौगोलिक विशेषताएँ, शैक्षणिक एवं सरकारी संस्थाएँ, खेल‑सुविधाएँ और प्राकृतिक आकर्षणों को आसानी से समझ सकें।

**मुख्य सामग्री**  

- **प्रशासनिक विभाजन**  
  - रांची जिला दो उपखंडों – रांची और बुंदू – में विभाजित है।  
  - कुल 18 ब्लॉक और 305 पंचायत शामिल हैं; रांची उपखंड में 14 ब्लॉक और बुंदू उपखंड में 4 ब्लॉक हैं।  

- **भौगोलिक एवं जलवायु विशेषताएँ**  
  - रांची झारखंड की राजधानी है और मध्यम जलवायु वाला शहर है।  
  - इसे “जलप्रपातों का शहर” के रूप में जाना जाता है।  

- **शैक्षणिक एवं सरकारी संस्थाएँ**  
  - प्रतिष्ठित शैक्षणिक संस्थान: बीआईटी मेसरा, आईआईएम, एनआईएफएफटी, एनयूएसआरएल, सीआईपी, आरआईएमएस आदि।  
  - महत्वपूर्ण सरकारी संगठनों के मुख्यालय: एचईसी, सीसीएल और सेल।  

- **खेल एवं मनोरंजन**  
  - विभिन्न खेलों के लिए अंतरराष्ट्रीय स्तर के स्टेडियम उपलब्ध हैं, जिससे शहर को गुणवत्तापूर्ण खेल बुनियादी ढाँचे के लिए पहचाना जाता है।  

- **प्राकृतिक पर्यटन**  
  - शहर से 70‑100 किमी की दूरी के भीतर कई झरने, पहाड़ियाँ और जंगल स्थित हैं, जो प्रकृति प्रेमियों के लिए आकर्षक गंतव्य बनाते हैं।  

संक्षेप में, यह पृष्ठ रांची जिले की प्रशासनिक संरचना, शैक्षणिक एवं सरकारी संसाधनों, खेल सुविधाओं और प्राकृतिक सौंदर्य का संक्षिप्त परिचय देता है, जिससे नागरिकों को अपने जिले के बारे में सटीक और उपयोगी जानकारी मिलती है।

knowledge_base.title: सार्वजनिक उपयोगिताएँ | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
The “सार्वजनिक उपयोगिताएँ” (Public Utilities) page on the Ranchi district website serves as a citizen‑friendly directory of essential services and institutions in Ranchi, Jharkhand. 

The page is organized into tabbed sections that group similar types of facilities:

* **अस्पताल (Hospitals)** – listings such as “अब्दुल रज्जाक अंसारी मेमोरियल अस्पताल” with its full address (इर्बा पोस्ट ऑफिस, एनएच 23, रुक्का, रांची एच ओ, रांची – 834001) and a contact phone number (+91 6512275717). Each hospital entry includes a Google‑Maps link for easy navigation.  
* **कॉलेज / विश्वविद्यालय (Colleges & Universities)** – entries provide the institution’s name, address and a map link.  
* **गैर‑सरकारी‑संगठन (NGOs)** – similar structured entries give the NGO’s name, location and map link.  
* **नगर‑पालिका (Municipal Bodies)** – details of local municipal offices, including address and map link.  
* **बिजली (Electricity)** – information on electricity offices or service centres.  
* **बैंक (Banks)** – bank branch names, addresses and map links.  
* **स्कूल (Schools)** – school names, addresses and map links.

Every listed item contains the entity’s name, a full postal address (often with PIN code), and a direct link to a Google‑Maps search that shows the exact location. Where available, contact telephone numbers are displayed (e.g., the hospital’s +91 6512275717).

knowledge_base.title: RANCHI - TOURIST PLACES | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as an official photo‑gallery showcase of Ranchi’s tourist attractions, maintained by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. Its goal is to give citizens and visitors a visual overview of the places they can explore in the district.

**Key content**  
The gallery presents a collection of images, each labelled with the name of the site and linked to a high‑resolution photograph hosted on the government’s CDN. The listed attractions are:

- Jagannath Temple Ranchi  
- Dewari Temple  
- Rajrappa Temple  
- Sun Temple  
- Pahari Temple  
- Hardeep Temple  
- Sai Temple  
- Birsa Zoo  
- Ranchi Aquarium  

For every item the page displays a thumbnail, the full‑size image URL, and the official title (e.g., “JAGGARNATH TEMPLE RANCHI”). Social‑share buttons (Facebook and X/Twitter) are provided so users can easily share individual images or the gallery page itself. The layout includes a breadcrumb trail (Home → Photo Gallery → Tourist Places), a heading (“Tourist Places”), and view‑switcher icons for grid or list presentation. No additional textual descriptions, contact details, or dates are included; the focus is purely on visual representation of the sites.

knowledge_base.title: दसम जलप्रपात | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is the official photo‑gallery for the Dasam Waterfall (दसम जलप्रपात) in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It presents the waterfall to citizens and visitors through a collection of images hosted on the government’s CDN.  

At the top of the page you find the page title “दसम जलप्रपात” and a breadcrumb trail showing the navigation path: Home → Photo Gallery → Dasam Waterfall.  

Below the title, a set of sharing tools lets users quickly post the page to Facebook or X (formerly Twitter). Adjacent to the sharing options are view‑switcher icons that allow the gallery to be displayed either as a grid or a list.  

The main content consists of a photo gallery section. Each gallery item includes a thumbnail of the waterfall, a caption (“दस्सम जलप्रपात”), and a link to the full‑size image. Clicking the thumbnail or the zoom icon opens the full photograph in a lightbox view, where the same sharing options are available for the individual image.  

Overall, the page serves as a visual showcase of the Dasam Waterfall, providing easy access to high‑quality pictures and simple ways for citizens to share the content on social media.

knowledge_base.title: Collectorate | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The Collectorate page for Ranchi district explains how the district’s general administration is organized and who is responsible for its day‑to‑day functioning. It states that the Deputy Commissioner serves as the executive head of the district, holding three intertwined roles—Deputy Commissioner, District Collector, and District Magistrate—each covering revenue, development, and law‑and‑order matters respectively. The page then lists the senior officers who assist the Deputy Commissioner in various sectors, including the Deputy Development Commissioner, ITDA Director, Additional Collector, Executive Magistrates, District Supply Officer, District Transport Officer, District Welfare Officer, District Panchayati Raj Officers, and Treasury Officer. It also notes that the Deputy Commissioner acts as the District Election Officer during elections and performs other specialized functions as needed. Overall, the page serves to inform citizens about the administrative structure of Ranchi district and the key officials who manage its governance, development, and public services.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page is a “Past Notices – Tenders” listing for the District Ranchi administration (Government of Jharkhand). It serves as an archive where citizens can view completed tender notices that were previously published by the district office.  

Each entry in the table shows:

* **Title of the tender** – e.g., “Blanket distribution program in social planning in Ranchi district,” “Under section 11 (1) of the land to be acquired in Bundu Upkarara Construction Project,” “Extension of last date of Proposal submission – RFP FOR DISTRICT MINERAL FOUNDATION (DMF) TRUST, RANCHI,” etc.  
* **Brief description** – often repeats the title or adds a note such as the date of the original RFP.  
* **Start and end dates** – the period during which the tender was open for submission (formatted as DD/MM/YYYY). For example, the blanket‑distribution tender ran from 06/12/2018 to 31/01/2019, while the social‑welfare department tender was open from 02/06/2018 to 06/06/2018.  
* **File link** – a direct PDF download (hosted on the government’s CDN) that contains the full tender document, with the file size shown in parentheses (e.g., “View (249 KB)”).  

The table includes six past tenders covering topics such as social‑welfare blankets, land acquisition for a construction project, proposals for the District Mineral Foundation Trust, and a notice from the Regional Transport Authority about publishing a vehicle timetable.  

Overall, the page provides a transparent record of completed procurement processes, allowing residents to see what projects were undertaken, when they were open for bids, and to access the original tender documents for reference or verification.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as a visual gallery that highlights the tourist attractions of Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It is intended for citizens and visitors who want to see photographs of notable sites in the area and get a quick sense of what the district offers for tourism.

**Key content presented**  

- A heading in Hindi: **“पर्यटक स्थल”** (Tourist Places).  
- A breadcrumb trail showing navigation: Home → फोटो गैलरी (Photo Gallery) → पर्यटक स्थल.  
- Social‑share buttons for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) to share the gallery page.  
- A view‑switcher that lets users toggle between a grid view and a list view of the images.  
- A series of image cards, each consisting of:  
  * A thumbnail picture of the site.  
  * The site’s name displayed in Hindi beneath the thumbnail (e.g., “जग्गरनाथ मंदिर रांची”, “हुंडरू फॉल्स”, “कanke dam”, etc.).  
  * A zoom‑icon link that opens the full‑size image in a lightbox view.  
  * Small Facebook and X icons inside each card for sharing that specific image.  

The structured data extracted from the page lists nine items, each with a Hindi title and a direct link to the full‑size photograph hosted on the government’s CDN. Examples of the titles (as they appear in the data) are:

Overall, the page is a straightforward, image‑focused directory of Ranchi’s tourist spots, providing citizens with an easy way to view and share pictures of the district’s attractions.

knowledge_base.title: DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | जलप्रपातों का शहर | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The homepage of the Ranchi district website (ranchi.nic.in/hi/) serves as an informational gateway for citizens, presenting a quick overview of the district’s geography, administration and notable features, and directing visitors to more detailed content.

**Key content visible on the page**

- **Visual banner carousel** – a rotating set of images at the top of the page that showcases various district‑related visuals (including an image labelled “SAFER INTERNET DAY – 11 FEB 2025”).  
- **About the district section** (“जिले के बारे में”) – a Hindi‑language description that explains:  
  * Ranchi is the capital of Jharkhand state.  
  * The district is divided into two subdivisions – Ranchi and Bundu – which together contain 18 blocks (14 in Ranchi subdivision, 4 in Bundu subdivision) and 305 panchayats.  
  * Ranchi has a moderate climate and was historically the summer capital of undivided Bihar.  
  * The city is popularly known as “जलप्रपातों का शहर” (the city of waterfalls).  
- **Call‑to‑action link** – a “अधिक पढ़ें” (Read more) button that leads to a deeper page about the district.  
- **Structural elements** – a separator line and the beginning of a tabbed interface (e.g., a “Whats‑new” tab) indicating that additional, timely updates are available elsewhere on the site.

Overall, the page is designed to give residents and visitors a concise snapshot of Ranchi’s administrative layout, climatic setting and cultural identity, while providing easy navigation to more detailed information and current notices.

knowledge.page_purpose:

The page provides citizens with an official overview of the administrative subdivisions and blocks that make up Ranchi district in Jharkhand. It is intended to help residents understand how the district is organized for governance and service delivery.

**Key content**  
- The page states that Ranchi district is divided into **two subdivisions**—**Ranchi** and **Bundu**—each overseen by a Sub‑Divisional Officer (SDO).  
- Under these subdivisions there are **eighteen blocks**, each administered by a Block Development Officer (BDO).

knowledge.page_purpose:

* **Bundu subdivision** – includes the blocks of Bundu, Rahe, Sonahatu, and Tamar.

Each row gives a serial number, the name of the subdivision, and the corresponding block name, allowing citizens to see which blocks belong to which subdivision within the district. The page’s purpose is to inform residents about the local administrative structure of Ranchi district.

knowledge_base.title: DEO PORTAL | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The DEO Portal for Ranchi district serves as a one‑stop source of election‑related information and resources for citizens, election officials and political contestants.  

At the top of the page a banner image and a heading (“DEO PORTAL”) introduce the site, followed by an embedded YouTube video of the Election Commission of India’s song “मैं भारत हूँ, हम भारत के मतदाता हैं” that is used for National Voters Day outreach.  

Below the video the page displays a series of tables that list the names of candidates who have filed nomination papers for various elections in Ranchi. Each entry shows the candidate’s name, their political party (or “Independent”), and a direct link to a PDF copy of their nomination document. The tables include contestants from parties such as Jharkhand Mukti Morcha, Communist Party of India (Marxist), Hamar Raj Party, Janata Dal (United), Jharkhand Loktantrik Krantikari Morcha, Bharat Adivasi Party, Jharkhand Party, Nationalist Congress Party, Bahujan Samaj Party, AJSU Party, Peoples Party of India, as well as numerous independent candidates.  

In addition to the candidate listings, the portal provides a collection of downloadable PDF assets that support voter education and election administration:

* A notice about National Voters Day 2025.  
* The press note for the Lok Sabha Election 2024.  
* Brochures for electors and for presiding officers on the use of EVMs and VVPATs.  
* Awareness material on the launch of the e‑Electoral Photo Identity Card (e‑EPIC).  
* Documentation for the Booth App used in Jharkhand.  
* Manuals for poll‑day monitoring software.  
* Standard operating procedures and training manuals for webcasting of polling stations.  

Together, these elements make the DEO Portal a practical hub where voters can learn about upcoming elections, verify candidate details, access instructional guides on voting technology, and stay informed about voter‑awareness initiatives.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an archive of past tender notices issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Government of Jharkhand. It allows citizens to view historical procurement opportunities that have been advertised by the district office.

Each entry in the table includes:

- **Title** – a brief description of the tender (e.g., “Regarding inviting tenders for purchase of material on the occasion of Lok Sabha election‑2019”, “Short term tender”, “REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP) FOR SELECTION OF AGENCY FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF PROJECT MANAGEMENT UNIT/ PLANNING & MONITORING UNIT [PMU] FOR INCREASING EFFICIENCY AND EFFECTIVENESS OF SCHEME ‘PRIME MINISTER AWAAS YOJANA – GRAMIN’”, etc.).
- **Description** – repeats the title or provides a short elaboration of the tender’s purpose.
- **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which the tender was open for submission (formatted as DD/MM/YYYY).
- **File** – links to the PDF documents associated with the notice, together with their file sizes (e.g., “View (6 MB)”, “View (141 KB)”, etc.), enabling users to download or view the original tender notice.

The listed tenders cover a variety of procurement needs, such as election‑related material supply, short‑term works, consultancy services for implementing the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana‑Gramin, blanket supply for the district, and other administrative notices. By presenting the title, dates, and downloadable files, the page provides a transparent record of past government procurement activities for public reference.

knowledge_base.title: परिवहन | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as the official information hub for the District Transport Office in Ranchi, Jharkhand. It informs citizens about where to access transport‑related services and provides the office’s contact details.

**Key details presented on the page**

- **Service description** – The page is titled “परिवहन” (Transport) and directs users to the national transport portal [parivahan.gov.in/parivahan/](https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan/) for broader services such as vehicle registration, driving licences, and permits.  
- **Office location** – Block B, District Transport Office, Room No‑316, situated in the Sammelanayalay (District Collectorate) building, Ranchi.  
- **Postal information** – PIN code 834001.  
- **Contact email** – dto‑ranchi[at]jharkhandmail[dot]gov[dot]in.  

The page does not list phone numbers or additional contact methods, but it gives the essential address, email, and a direct link to the central Parivahan portal, enabling residents to find further assistance or complete transport‑related transactions online.

knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as an archive of past official notices and announcements issued by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It presents each notice in a table format that includes:

- **Title** – a brief headline of the announcement (e.g., “Result of NHM Advertisement No. 1/2025”, “Birth and Death Registration – November 2025”, “Construction of Tableaux for Republic Day 2026 at Morhabadi Maidan”).  
- **Description** – a short summary of what the notice covers, such as press releases, office orders, lists of selected candidates, monthly vital‑statistics reports, or invitations for specific events.  
- **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which the notice was valid or the event was scheduled (e.g., 18 Jan 2026 – 17 Feb 2026 for the NHM result, 27 Dec 2025 – 27 Jan 2026 for birth‑death registration).  
- **File** – a downloadable PDF (size indicated) that contains the full document, accessible via a direct link.

The notices span a range of government activities: health‑sector updates from the National Health Mission, vital‑statistics reporting, cultural‑event preparations (Republic Day tableaux), recruitment or selection outcomes, and other administrative announcements. By providing the title, description, dates, and PDF links, the page enables citizens to easily locate and review past official communications from the Ranchi district administration.

knowledge_base.title: Feedback | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The page serves as the official feedback channel for the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand). It invites residents to submit their comments, suggestions, or concerns directly to the district office through an embedded contact form. The layout includes a clear “Feedback” heading, a ready‑to‑use Contact Form 7 widget (form ID 24060) for submitting messages, and social‑share buttons that let users share the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter). Navigation aids such as a breadcrumb trail (Home → Feedback) and a banner image at the top help users orient themselves within the district website. The page’s sole focus is to facilitate two‑way communication between citizens and the Ranchi district government.

knowledge_base.title: कमजोर एवं अभिवंचित परिवार के छात्रों हेतु RTE Act के तहत मान्यता प्राप्त निजी विद्यालयों के प्रारम्भिक कक्षाओं की 25% आरक्षित सीटों पर नामांकन हेतु सूचना | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose: This notice from the Ranchi district administration informs citizens about the admission process for the 25 % of seats reserved in recognized private schools under the Right‑to‑Education (RTE) Act for children from economically weaker and disadvantaged families. It specifies that applications for these reserved seats in the pre‑primary classes can be submitted between 14 February 2026 and 10 April 2026, and provides a downloadable PDF (≈2 MB) containing the detailed guidelines and application form. The page is intended to help eligible families understand the procedure and timelines for securing a place in participating private schools.

knowledge_base.title: Transport | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: The page provides information about the Transport service offered by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It directs citizens to the national Parivahan portal (https://parivahan.gov.in/parivahan/) for vehicle‑related services and lists the local office details where transport‑related matters can be handled in person. The office is located in Block B, District Transport Office, Room 316 of the Collectorate Building in Ranchi, with PIN 834001. For electronic communication, the designated email address is dto‑ranchi@jharkhandmail.gov.in. The page also includes standard social‑media sharing links for Facebook and X (formerly Twitter). No additional contact numbers or directory listings are presented.

knowledge_base.title: जिले के बारे में | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page provides an overview of Ranchi district for citizens and visitors, explaining its administrative structure, key features, and attractions.

**Key content covered**

- **Administrative layout** – Ranchi is the capital of Jharkhand and is divided into two subdivisions (Ranchi and Bundu). These subdivisions contain a total of 18 blocks and 305 panchayats, with 14 blocks in the Ranchi subdivision and 4 blocks in the Bundu subdivision.  
- **Geographic and climatic notes** – Ranchi enjoys a moderate climate and has historically served as the summer capital of undivided Bihar. It is popularly known as the “City of Waterfalls.”  
- **Educational hub** – The city attracts students from Bihar, Jharkhand, and parts of West Bengal for senior secondary and higher education. Notable institutions mentioned include BIT Mesra, IIM, NIFT, NUSRL, CIP, and RIMS.  
- **Government presence** – Important offices of central and state‑level organisations such as HEC, CCL, and SAIL are located in Ranchi.  
- **Sports infrastructure** – Ranchi is recognised for its quality sports facilities, hosting international‑standard stadiums for various games.  
- **Tourism and natural attractions** – Within a 70‑100 km radius of the city lie numerous waterfalls, hills, and forests, making it a destination for nature lovers.  

Overall, the page serves as a concise guide to Ranchi’s administrative divisions, educational and governmental significance, recreational amenities, and natural beauty.

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
This page serves as an archive of past recruitment notices issued by the Ranchi district administration (Government of Jharkhand). It allows citizens to view, download, and refer to official notices, advertisements, corrigenda, and related documents for various government‑job selections that have already been announced or completed.

**Key content**  

| Notice / Advertisement | What it covers | Important dates (start – end) | Associated files (size) |
|------------------------|----------------|------------------------------|--------------------------|
| Teacher Recruitment Counseling 2024 – final selected candidates | Letter and list of candidates finally selected for teacher posts | 23 Sep 2024 – 27 Sep 2024 | View (815 KB) |
| Teacher Recruitment Counseling 2024 – to be held on 08/09/2024 | Letter, selected‑list, counselling schedule and related documents | 06 Sep 2024 – 08 Sep 2024 | View (191 KB), Selected candidate for Counselling dated 08092024 (1 MB), Aaapti Nirakaran (2 MB) |
| Advertisement for direct recruitment of Chowkidar cadre (Ranchi district) | Recruitment notice for Chowkidar positions | 09 Aug 2024 – 25 Aug 2024 | View (8 MB) |
| Corrigendum for direct recruitment of Chowkidar cadre | Corrections/updates to the Chowkidar advertisement | 12 Aug 2024 – 25 Aug 2024 | View (371 KB) |
| Claim/Objection for Teacher Recruitment Counseling 2024 (held 01/08/2024) | Facility for candidates to raise objections/claims after the counselling held on 01 Aug 2024 | 22 Aug 2024 – 25 Aug 2024 | View (394 KB), Candidate list (6 MB) |
| Important information regarding recruitment against vacant posts of Account Assistant & Computer Assistant under MGNREGA | Details on eligibility test, merit‑list objections for MGNREGA‑linked posts | 21 Aug 2024 – 24 Aug 2024 | View (397 KB), Computer Assistants (277 KB), Account Assistant (266 KB) |
| Teacher Appointment Councelling 2024 – for Objection | Notice inviting objections during the teacher appointment counselling process | 10 Aug 2024 – 17 Aug 2024 | View (2 MB) |
| Teacher Appointment Counseling 2024 – Selected List of Candidates | Final list of candidates appointed to teacher posts | 01 Aug 2024 – 15 Aug 2024 | View (… KB) – link provided in the table |
| Account Assistant and Computer Assistant under MGNREGA (Eligibility Test) | Information publication for the eligibility test for these MGNREGA posts (advertisement No. 1235(ii)/…, dated 19/10/2023) | – (notice date Mar 2024) | View (… KB) – link provided |
| Notice Regarding GRS recruitment | List related to contract‑based appointment of Gram Rozgar Sevak under MNREGA | – (notice date Mar 2024) | View (… KB) – link provided |

**What a citizen can do**  
- Click any “View (…)” link to open or download the corresponding PDF notice.  
- Refer to the start and end dates to understand the period during which the notice was active or objections were invited.

knowledge_base.title: Social Security | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as the official information hub for the Social Security Pension service offered by the District Administration of Ranchi, Jharkhand. It informs citizens about where to access the service and provides essential contact details.

Key information presented includes:

- **Service description**: The page is titled “Social Security” and indicates that it pertains to the social‑security pension scheme administered by the district office.
- **Visit link**: A direct URL to the national social‑security pension portal (http://nsap.nic.in/) is provided for further information or online services.
- **Office location**: The District Social Security Office is located in Block A, Room No‑315 of the Collectrate Building, Collectorate Office, Ranchi, with the PIN code 834001.
- **Navigation aids**: Breadcrumb trail shows the page’s place within the site (Home → Services → Social Security) and social‑share buttons allow users to share the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

Overall, the page directs residents to the appropriate office and online resource for availing or learning about social‑security pension benefits in Ranchi district.

knowledge_base.title: DMFT Ranchi | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The Ranchi District Mineral Foundation Trust (DMFT) page serves as the official information hub for the trust that manages funds collected from mining activities in the district. It introduces the trust’s mandate and structure under the “About Us” tab, then provides easy access to the legal and regulatory framework that governs its operations—including the Jharkhand DMFT Rules, the PMKKKY Guidelines, the trust’s constitution, subsequent amendments, the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Bill 2021, and a notice on restrictions for drinking‑water projects under DMFT.

Visitors can also explore the trust’s programmatic work through the “DMFT Ranchi Schemes” section, which hosts downloadable scheme‑detail documents for the financial years 2022‑23 down to 2019‑20, outlining the projects and initiatives funded in each year. Additional tabs cover the geographic areas affected by mining that benefit from DMFT interventions, the specific sectors (such as health, education, livelihood, and infrastructure) in which the trust invests, and the annual reports that summarize achievements and financial statements.

Overall, the page consolidates key documents, scheme details, and sector‑wise information, giving citizens a transparent view of how DMFT Ranchi utilizes mining revenues for local development.

The entries are displayed in a structured format with headings and bullet‑point lists, making it easy for users to scan the names, locate the addresses, and click through to the institutions’ websites for further details such as courses offered, admission procedures, contact numbers, and other relevant information. knowledge_base.title: Past  | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page is the “Past Notices → Tenders” section of the Ranchi district website (Government of Jharkhand). Its purpose is to give citizens easy access to tender notices that have already been issued, allowing them to see what procurement opportunities were advertised, when they were open, and to download the related documents.

The page displays a table of ten past tenders, each row showing:

* **Title** – a brief identifier (e.g., “Short‑term Tender 1518(ii) 13.12.21”, “Revised Tender Notice No. DHS/RAN/2021‑22 under District Health Society,Ranchi”, “EOI Telemedicine for Sonahatu of Ranchi District”).  
* **Description** – a short elaboration of the tender’s subject.  
* **Start Date** and **End Date** – the period during which bids were invited (all dates fall between June 2021 and December 2021).  
* **File** – one or more PDF documents (hosted on the government’s CDN `cdn.s3waas.gov.in`) that contain the full tender notice, specifications, or related annexes. File sizes range from about 135 KB to 5 MB.

The tenders cover a variety of district‑level departments and schemes, including:

* District Health Society (revised notices, very short tenders, telemedicine EOI, oxygen‑plant procurement).  
* Labour Department (short‑term tender).  
* District Rural Development Agency (short‑term tender).  
* MGNREGA‑related work (Birsa Harit Gram Yojna).  
* General administration (home‑isolation medicine kit requirement).

By presenting the title, dates, and direct download links, the page enables residents, businesses, and interested parties to review what the district has previously procured, understand the timelines involved, and access the original tender documents for reference or compliance purposes.

knowledge_base.title: Village & Panchayats | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The “Village & Panchayats” page on the Ranchi District website provides citizens with an overview of the administrative subdivisions (blocks) within Ranchi district, showing how many gram panchayats and revenue villages each block contains. It also gives the district‑wide totals for panchayats and villages, helping residents understand the local governance structure and the geographic spread of villages across the district.

**Key content presented**

- **District‑wide summary** – Ranchi District has **1,311 revenue villages** organized under **305 panchayats**.
- **Block‑wise table** – For each of the 18 blocks in the district, the page lists:
  - Serial number (S. NO.)
  - Block name (e.g., Angara, Bero, Bundu, …, Tamar)
  - Number of panchayats in that block
  - Number of revenue villages in that block  

  Example entries:  
  - Angara – 21 panchayats, 83 villages  
  - Bero – 17 panchayats, 84 villages  
  - Bundu – 11 panchayats, 88 villages  
  - …  
  - Tamar – 23 panchayats, 144 villages  

The table allows residents to quickly see how their block compares with others in terms of local governance units and village count, facilitating awareness of the district’s administrative layout.

knowledge_base.title: बिजली |  DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND  | भारत

knowledge.page_purpose:
This page serves as a directory of electricity‑related public utilities in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It presents three key organizations that citizens can contact for services, complaints, or information about power supply and regulation:

1. **Jharkhand State Electricity Regulatory Commission (JSEERC)** – provides the regulatory oversight for electricity in the state. The entry includes its full address (विपक्षी चर्च परिसर, महात्मा गांधी मुख्य आरडी, हिंदिपी, रांची, झारखंड 834001) and a link to its website (http://jserc.org/).

2. **Jharkhand Bijli Vitran Nigam Limited (JBVNL)** – the state‑owned distribution company responsible for delivering electricity to consumers. The listing gives its address (एचबी रोड, कोकर, रांची, झारखंड 834001) and directs users to its website (http://jbvnl.co.in/).

3. **Jharkhand State Electricity Board** – the historic board that continues to be referenced for electricity matters. Its address is shown (झारखंड राज्य बिजली बोर्ड, एस.एन. रोड, रांची, झारखंड 834001) together with a map link for location details.

Each entry provides a clickable map link (via Google Maps) showing the exact location and a direct website link for further information or online services. The page is designed to help residents quickly find contact details, addresses, and online resources for the main electricity service providers and regulator in Ranchi.

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as a document repository for the Ranchi district administration, allowing citizens to view and download official PDFs that are relevant to public information and services.  

It currently hosts three downloadable files:

1. **EVM Brochure for Electors on use of EVM and VVPAT** – dated 18 December 2023, about 10 MB in size. This brochure explains how voters can use Electronic Voting Machines and the Voter‑Verified Paper Audit Trail system.  

2. **Regarding Brochure for Presiding Officers on use EVM and VVPAT** – also dated 18 December 2023, roughly 4 MB. This guide is intended for polling‑station officers, detailing their responsibilities when operating EVMs and VVPATs during elections.  

3. **Census Data 2011** – dated 27 April 2018, approximately 4 MB. This document provides the district‑level statistics from the 2011 Census of India for Ranchi.  

Each entry includes a direct “View / Download” link that opens the PDF in a new window, together with the file size so users know what to expect before downloading. The page also offers a simple filter (via a dropdown) to narrow the list to the “Census Data 2011” category or show all documents, and includes standard social‑media sharing options for the page itself. Overall, the page is a straightforward, citizen‑focused portal for accessing key electoral and demographic documents issued by the Ranchi district government.

knowledge_base.title: Hundru Waterfall | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
The page serves as an official tourism guide for Hundru Waterfall, a major attraction in Ranchi district, Jharkhand. It introduces the waterfall as one of the city’s most popular destinations, noting that it is formed where the Subarnarekha River drops about 320 feet, making it the highest waterfall in the state. The description highlights the natural pool at the base that is used for bathing and picnics, the scenic rock formations created by water erosion, and the surrounding Chota Nagpur Plateau views that draw visitors.

Practical information is provided under a “How to Reach” section, which explains that the falls lie roughly 45 kilometres from Ranchi’s city centre and are accessible via the Ranchi‑Purulia Road. A direction button links to Google Maps for navigation assistance.

The page also features a photo gallery showcasing images of the waterfall, with a link to view the full collection. Social‑share tools allow visitors to share the page on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter), and a breadcrumb trail shows the site’s hierarchy (Home → Tourist Places → Hundru Waterfall). Overall, the content aims to inform citizens and tourists about the waterfall’s natural appeal, visitor amenities, and how to get there.

knowledge_base.title: History | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose:
**Purpose of the page**  
The page serves as a concise historical overview of Ranchi district, highlighting key events and movements that shaped its social and political development. It is intended for citizens who want to learn about the district’s role in India’s freedom struggle and its local socio‑economic history.

**Key content covered**

- **Non‑Cooperation Movement in Ranchi** – Describes how the movement resonated locally, especially among the Tana Bhagats, who participated in the 1922 Gaya Congress session, spread the freedom message through barefoot treks, attended a khadi exhibition inaugurated by Rajendra Prasad in 1926, and later supported the Civil Disobedience and Salt Satyagraha campaigns. It also notes the district’s involvement in the Quit India movement of 1942, including strikes, processions, and disruptions to communications.

- **Main Events after 1857** – Outlines the agrarian unrest that led to the Sardari (Larai) agitation against forced labor and rent hikes, culminating in the emergence of Birsa Munda around 1895. Birsa Munda is portrayed as a socio‑religious leader whose half‑agrarian, half‑religious movement earned him the title “Birsa Bhagwan” and left a lasting imprint on Ranchi’s social history.

Together, these sections give readers a clear picture of Ranchi’s participation in national freedom struggles and its distinctive local movements rooted in agrarian and tribal concerns.

knowledge_base.title: Regarding publication of Section-11 | DISTRICT RANCHI, GOVERNMENT OF JHARKHAND | India

knowledge.page_purpose: This notice from the District Ranchi office announces the publication of Section 11 for a road‑development project. It concerns the construction of a four‑lane road (including bridges) that will run from Argora (Old Argora Chowk) on MDR‑08 to Naya Sarai (Ring Road) on MDR‑013, passing through Pundag Path. The total length of the project is 6.175 km. The notice specifies that the work is scheduled to begin on 28 January 2026 and to be completed by 28 March 2026. A detailed PDF document (approximately 2 MB) containing the full Section 11 publication is available for viewing or download via the provided link. This information is intended to keep citizens informed about the upcoming infrastructure work and its timeline.
